- FIXED: `CY-A` scope leaks. In the touched Vol III chapter files, `d = 2` now cites `Theorem~CY-A$_2$`, while `d = 3` now uses `Conjecture~CY-A$_3$` or explicit programme language. - FIXED: stale MC3 wording. References that said MC3 held “for all simple types” were narrowed to the live cross-volume scope: the evaluation-generated core. The main fixes are in [quantum_group_reps.tex](/Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/examples/quantum_group_reps.tex) and [introduction.tex](/Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/theory/introduction.tex).
- FIXED: `kappa` subscript conflation. In [cy_to_chiral.tex](/Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex), the genus-1 discussion now distinguishes the shadow scalar `\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24` from the automorphic/BKM coefficient `\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24`, and removes the `\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}` conflation.
- BLOCKED: none.

**Verification**

A repo-wide `rg` over `chapters/` shows no remaining hits for these stale patterns:

- `Theorem~CY-A at $d = 3`
- `Theorem~CY-A$_3$`

There are still many `Vol~I` / `Vol~II` citations in `chapters/`, but on this pass the stale ones tied to `CY-A` scope and `kappa` subscripts were the ones corrected. I did not run a full LaTeX build; verification was by source reread plus targeted grep.

chapters/examples/toric_cy3_coha.tex:6:For $d = 2$, the question would be settled by Theorem~CY-A$_2$ directly. For $d = 3$, it is the programme: $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ is conditional on the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing, so any claim about the resulting chiral algebra must be tagged accordingly. What is unconditional is the CoHA side. The toric diagram of $X_\Sigma$ determines a quiver with potential $(Q_X, W_X)$; the critical CoHA is $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X) = \bigoplus_\mathbf{d} H^{\mathrm{BM}}_*(\mathrm{Crit}(W_\mathbf{d}), \phi_{W_\mathbf{d}})$; the theorems of Schiffmann--Vasserot ($\C^3$) and Rapcak--Soibelman--Yang--Zhao (general toric CY3 without compact $4$-cycles) identify $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X)$ with the positive half $Y^+(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X})$ of the affine super Yangian attached to the toric quiver.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:4853: \item \textbf{(Conjecture.)} There exists a chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X)))$ whose bar complex $B(A)$ encodes the product formula for $\Delta_5$. \emph{Note}: the functor $\Phi$ is constructed for $d = 2$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$); the $d = 3$ case (which applies here, since $K3 \times E$ is CY$_3$) is the content of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:4953:The abstract functor $\Phi \colon D^b(\Coh(X)) \to \text{ChirAlg}$ of Conjecture CY-A$_3$ can be bypassed for $K3 \times E$ by exploiting the elliptic fibration $\pi \colon S \to \bP^1$ directly.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:5130: \item \textbf{CY$_3$ quantum vertex chiral group (Conjecture CY-A$_3$).} Apply the abstract CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi \colon D^b(\Coh(K3 \times E)) \to \text{ChirAlg}$ of Conjecture CY-A$_3$. This gives $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\Coh(K3 \times E)))$ directly, with $G(K3 \times E) = G(A_{K3 \times E})$. \emph{Status}: conjectural (CY-A$_3$ is not yet proved; the $d=2$ case CY-A$_2$ is proved).
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:5137:Routes 1--3 give progressively more detailed structural information: Route 1 captures the combinatorial skeleton ($\fn_+$ and the root system), Route 2 gives the generic (abelian) fiber, and Route 3 gives the enhanced (nonabelian) fiber. Route 4 would give the full chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ but requires CY-A$_3$. Route 5 gives the physical interpretation but not a rigorous construction. The relative chiral algebra of Section~\ref{sec:k3e-relative-chiral} provides a concrete intermediary between Routes 2--3 and Route 4.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:5429: \item The \emph{shadow MC element} $\Theta_A \in \mathrm{MC}(\mathrm{Def}_{\mathrm{cyc}}^{\mathrm{mod}}(A))$ of the chiral algebra $A = A_{K3 \times E}$ (conditional on Conjecture CY-A$_3$), in the modular cyclic deformation complex of Volume~I.

chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:67:a two-dimensional $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on one generator. The Jacobi ring is $\Jac(x^2) = \C[x]/(2x) \cong \C$, so $\HH_\bullet(\MF(x^2)) \cong \C$ is one-dimensional; the two-dimensional endomorphism algebra counts the $\Z/2$-graded indecomposable and its parity shift. The dimension count $2$ is the rank count of the free-fermion representation: a single holomorphic fermion contributes a two-dimensional Clifford sector, and this is the smallest nonzero input to the LG-to-chiral passage at the level of Hochschild invariants. The $n = 1$ case sits outside the CY$_2$ domain of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Remark~\ref{rem:mf-dim-caveat}) and is used only as the building block for the stabilized four-variable model in Section~\ref{sec:ade-w-algebras}.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:72:Let $W \colon \C^4 \to \C$ have an isolated critical point at the origin. By Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} the category $\MF(W)$ is CY$_2$, hence Theorem~CY-A$_2$ applies and produces a chiral algebra
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:81:Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}) applies to any smooth proper CY$_2$ category. The value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi(\HH_\bullet)$ is the CY Euler characteristic clause of that theorem; Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-hh} identifies $\HH_\bullet$ with $\Jac(W)$ placed in a single parity, whose Euler characteristic is the $\C$-dimension of the Jacobi ring, namely $\mu(W)$.

chapters/theory/cyclic_ainf.tex:4:A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.

chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:67:a two-dimensional $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on one generator. The Jacobi ring is $\Jac(x^2) = \C[x]/(2x) \cong \C$, so $\HH_\bullet(\MF(x^2)) \cong \C$ is one-dimensional; the two-dimensional endomorphism algebra counts the $\Z/2$-graded indecomposable and its parity shift. The dimension count $2$ is the rank count of the free-fermion representation: a single holomorphic fermion contributes a two-dimensional Clifford sector, and this is the smallest nonzero input to the LG-to-chiral passage at the level of Hochschild invariants. The $n = 1$ case sits outside the CY$_2$ domain of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Remark~\ref{rem:mf-dim-caveat}) and is used only as the building block for the stabilized four-variable model in Section~\ref{sec:ade-w-algebras}.

\section{The CY$_3$ collision residue}
\label{sec:cy3-collision-residue}

The collision residue is a single algebraic operation extracted from the
universal Maurer--Cartan element $\Theta_A$ of a chiral algebra. In the
CY setting, this operation acquires geometric meaning: the residue is
computed against the holomorphic CY volume form, and the resulting binary
operation is the classical limit of the quantum vertex chiral group
braiding.

\begin{definition}[Binary collision residue, CY version]
\label{def:cy-collision-residue}
Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper Calabi--Yau category of dimension~$d$ with
holomorphic volume form $\Omega_\cC$. Let $A_\cC$ denote the chiral
algebra produced by the CY-to-chiral functor (Theorem~CY-A$_d$, proved

The Vol~III main conjecture CY-C upgrades Theorem~\ref{thm:qgf-kazhdan-lusztig} from affine Kac--Moody data to CY-category data. We recall the precise statement and scope.

\begin{conjecture}[CY-C: quantum group realization]
\label{conj:qgf-cy-c}
\ClaimStatusConjectured
Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper CY-$d$ category ($d = 2$ or $d = 3$) satisfying the hypotheses of Theorem~CY-A$_d$ (Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral}), and let $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ be its image under the CY-to-chiral functor. There exists a parameter $q \in \C^*$ (determined by $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC)$) and a Hopf algebra in a braided category, $C(\cC, q)$, such that there is an equivalence of modular tensor categories
\begin{equation}
 \Rep^{\mathrm{fd}}(C(\cC, q)) \ \simeq \ \mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)),
 \label{eq:qgf-cy-c}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)) := \Rep^{\Etwo}(A_\cC)^{\mathrm{ss}}$ is the (semisimplified) conformal-block category of $A_\cC$. When $\cC = D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(\mathrm{pt}/G))$ for $G$ a simple algebraic group, one recovers $C(\cC, q) = U_q(\mathrm{Lie}\, G)$ and Theorem~\ref{thm:qgf-kazhdan-lusztig}.
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Scope by CY dimension]
\label{rem:qgf-cy-c-scope}
Conjecture~\ref{conj:qgf-cy-c} is stated as a single conjecture but splits by dimension:
\begin{itemize}
 \item $d = 2$ (K$3$ and abelian surface cases): the functor $\Phi$ is constructed (Theorem~CY-A$_2$), so $A_\cC$ is defined, and CY-C reduces to a semisimplification plus reconstruction statement. The BKM route (Borcherds--Gritsenko--Nikulin) provides partial evidence; see Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e} and the toroidal elliptic computation in Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}. At $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} = \chi(\cO_{K3}) = 2$, while $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ is the weight of $\Delta_5$: forbids conflation.
 \item $d = 3$: the functor $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ is conditional on the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing. Conjecture~\ref{conj:qgf-cy-c} at $d = 3$ is therefore doubly conjectural: it depends on CY-A$_3$, and assuming CY-A$_3$ it further conjectures the existence of $C(\cC, q)$. The only end-to-end verified case is $\C^3$, where $C(\C^3, q)$ is the affine Yangian $Y^+(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1)$ (Schiffmann--Vasserot, Kontsevich--Soibelman).
 succeeded in 50ms:
\chapter{Modular Koszul Duality and CY Geometry}
\label{ch:modular-koszul-bridge}

A CY category $\cC$ produces, via the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ of Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral}, a chiral algebra $A_\cC$; the bar complex $B(A_\cC) = T^c(s^{-1}\overline{A_\cC})$, built on the augmentation ideal $\overline{A_\cC} = \ker(\varepsilon)$, is a factorization coalgebra on $\Ran(C)$. Three Volume~I structures act on $B(A_\cC)$. The Verdier intertwining $D_{\Ran}(B(A)) \simeq B(A^!)$ of Theorem~A is a functor of factorization coalgebras on $\Ran(C)$; it is the Koszul duality, not bar-cobar inversion, and not the chiral derived center. Complementarity (Theorem~C) splits the genus-$g$ shadow complex into Verdier eigenspaces and, on the uniform-weight lane, equates the scalar sum of Koszul-dual modular characteristics to a family-dependent Koszul conductor. The genus tower (Theorem~D) identifies $\mathrm{obs}_g$ with $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} \cdot \lambda_g$ on the uniform-weight lane at genus $1$ unconditionally, with a cross-channel correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ at $g \geq 2$ for multi-weight algebras. Vol~III inherits three deficiencies. First, the convolution dg Lie algebra living on $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$ has no existing CY-side habitat. Second, the Vol~I scalar complementarity (Vol~I Theorem~C$_2$, with its family-dependent Koszul conductor; see Remark~\ref{rem:cy-complementarity-kappa-zero} below) has no CY translation stating which Koszul conductor $K_X$ applies at $d \in \{2, 3\}$. Third, the Vol~I CohFT promotion (Theorem~D$+$H) has no CY restatement tracking the flat identity axiom through $\Phi$. Five sections address these deficiencies and their consequences: \S\ref{sec:modular-conv-cy} builds the CY modular convolution algebra; \S\ref{sec:cy-complementarity-bridge} transports complementarity with explicit (C1) versus (C2) scoping and explicit $d = 2$ versus $d = 3$ conditionality; \S\ref{sec:cy-shadow-cohft} upgrades the shadow tower to a CohFT on $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$ and records how the Borcherds lift converts the $K3 \times E$ tower into the genus-$2$ Igusa cusp form $\Phi_{10}$; \S\ref{sec:hochschild-bridge} establishes the bridge between the three Hochschild theories (categorical, chiral, derived-center) through $\Phi$; and \S\ref{sec:cy-bridge-examples} collects the principal examples with their $\kappa_\bullet$-spectra.


%% ===================================================================
%% SECTION 1: CY modular convolution algebra
%% ===================================================================

\section{The modular convolution algebra for CY categories}
\label{sec:modular-conv-cy}

Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper cyclic $A_\infty$-category of CY dimension $d$ and let $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ denote the image under the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ of Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral} (Theorem~CY-A is proved for $d = 2$; $d = 3$ is the Vol~III programme, AP-CY6). The bar coalgebra $B(A_\cC)$ is a factorization coalgebra on $\Ran(C)$ for a fixed smooth projective curve $C$, with bar differential $d_B = d_1 + d_2 + \cdots$ where $d_k$ lowers bar degree by $k - 1$.

\begin{definition}[CY modular convolution algebra]
\label{def:cy-modular-convolution}
The \emph{CY modular convolution algebra} of the pair $(B(A_\cC), A_\cC)$ is the graded vector space
\[
 \mathrm{Conv}_{\mathrm{str}}\bigl(B(A_\cC), A_\cC\bigr)
 succeeded in 51ms:

\item \textbf{Toric CY$_3$: proved via the CoHA.}
For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The correspondence holds at the motivic level because the CoHA multiplication IS the bar differential, and the CoHA Hilbert series IS the bar Euler characteristic.

\item \textbf{$K3 \times E$: observational at genus~$1$, motivic level requires $\Phi_3$.}
The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The shadow data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)/1$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The genus-$1$ shadow $F_1 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$ matches the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.

\item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}
The quintic has $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = -25/3$ (this is one of the cases where $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24$ holds; Theorem~\ref{thm:chi-neq-kappa}). The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches. The BCOV holomorphic anomaly equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:hae-mc-structural}) provides the higher-genus comparison, but the existence of $A_X$ as an $\Eone$-chiral algebra for the quintic depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.

\end{enumerate}
\end{remark}

\section{The quantum chiral algebra of a Fukaya category}
\label{sec:fukaya-qca}

\begin{construction}[Assembling $\Phi(\Fuk(X))$]
\label{constr:fukaya-phi}
For a CY manifold $X$ of dimension $d$, the quantum chiral algebra
$A_{\Fuk(X)} = \Phi(\Fuk(X))$ is assembled in three steps
(matching the abstract construction of Theorem~CY-A):
\begin{enumerate}[label=\arabic*.]
 \item \emph{Cyclic $\Ainf$-extraction}: The Fukaya $\Ainf$-structure
 $\{\mu_k\}_{k \geq 1}$ and Poincar\'{e} pairing give the input
 cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra (Chapter~\ref{ch:cyclic-ainf});
 \item \emph{Hochschild-to-chiral bridge}: The categorical Hochschild
 cochains $\mathrm{CC}^\bullet(\Fuk(X), \Fuk(X))$ carry the
 Gerstenhaber bracket and Connes operator; under the bar dictionary
 these map to the chiral Hochschild cochains of the output chiral
 algebra, and on cohomology give the Lie conformal algebra
 $L_{\Fuk(X)}$ (Chapter~\ref{ch:hochschild-calculus} and
 Proposition~\ref{prop:fukaya-hochschild-bridge});
 \item \emph{Factorization envelope}: The factorization envelope
 succeeded in 51ms:
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Well-definedness for general CY$_3$]
\label{rem:fact-coprod-general-cy3}
The factorization-homology coproduct $\Delta^{\mathrm{Ran}}$ is
well-defined for \emph{any} CY$_3$ category $\cC$ for which the
CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ produces a factorization algebra---i.e.,
whenever CY-A holds.
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
  \item \emph{At $d = 2$ (unconditional):} For a CY$_2$ category
  (e.g.\ $D^b(\Coh(K3))$), the functor $\Phi$ produces an
  $\Etwo$-chiral algebra (Theorem~CY-A at $d = 2$), and the
  factorization-homology coproduct is the excision map for any
  collar-gluing of the underlying surface.  This recovers the
  lattice vertex algebra coproduct (primitive on generators)
  without any toric structure.
  \item \emph{At $d = 3$ (conditional on CY-A$_3$):} The
  functor $\Phi$ is conjectural, but if it produces a factorization
  algebra $\cF$ on a curve $C$, then the excision coproduct is
  automatically well-defined.  The Miura factorization is the

This product has the \emph{form} of a bar-complex Euler characteristic
(each factor $(1 - p^n q^l r^m)^{f(D)}$ corresponds to a putative
bar generator of dimension vector $(n, l, m)$ with multiplicity
$|f(D)|$), but the identification of $\Delta_5$ with the bar Euler
product of a chiral algebra requires both the $d = 3$ CY-to-chiral
functor (Conjecture~CY-A$_3$) and the Vol~I bar Euler product
machinery applied to $A_{K3 \times E}$. At $d = 2$, the analogous
identification is a theorem (CY-A$_2$); at $d = 3$ it is an
observation matching the formal structure, not a proved identity
(AP-CY8: the Borcherds denominator is not the bar Euler product without the CY-to-chiral functor).
\ClaimStatusConditional{Product formula: proved (Gritsenko--Nikulin). Bar-Euler-product identification: conditional on CY-A$_3$.}
\end{proposition}

\begin{remark}[Shadow depth: class M]
\label{rem:k3e-holographic-shadow-depth}
The $K3 \times E$ holographic datum has shadow depth $r_{\max} = \infty$
(class M in the shadow depth classification of
Definition~\ref{def:shadow-depth-classification}).
 succeeded in 51ms:
\label{thm:k3e-yau-zaslow}
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
The genus-$0$ BPS invariants $n_h^0$ of $K3$ satisfy
\[
 \sum_{h \geq 0} n_h^0 \, q^h = \prod_{k \geq 1} \frac{1}{(1 - q^k)^{24}} = \frac{q}{\eta(q)^{24}},
\]
giving $n_0^0 = 1$, $n_1^0 = 24$, $n_2^0 = 324$, $n_3^0 = 3200$, $n_4^0 = 25650$, $n_5^0 = 176256$. Equivalently, $n_h^0 = \chi(\mathrm{Hilb}^h(K3))$.
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
\end{theorem}

This is proved by Beauville (1999) via deformation to the Hilbert scheme and by Bryan--Leung (2000) via symplectic techniques. The BPS integrality $n_h^g \in \Z$ for all genera is proved by Pandharipande--Thomas (2016).

\begin{proposition}[BPS / shadow tower comparison]
\label{prop:k3e-bps-shadow}
The Yau--Zaslow generating function and the shadow obstruction tower of $A_{K3,\mathrm{rel}}$ are related by:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
 \item \textup{(Proved.)} At genus~$0$: $n_h^0 = \chi(\mathrm{Hilb}^h(K3))$ counts rational curves, while $F_0 = 0$ (the genus-$0$ shadow obstruction vanishes by definition of the shadow tower, which starts at $g \geq 1$).
 \item \textup{(Proved.)} At genus~$1$: the shadow free energy $F_1 = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} / 24 = 2/24 = 1/12$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:k3-kappa}) matches the genus-$1$ Gromov--Witten contribution $\sum_h N_{1,h}^{\mathrm{red}} \, Q^h$ via the KKV formula, after extracting the $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$-dependent piece.
 \item \textup{(Conditional on CY-A$_3$.)} At genus~$g \geq 2$: $F_g = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} \cdot \lambda_g^{\mathrm{FP}} = 2 \cdot \lambda_g^{\mathrm{FP}}$ captures the tautological class contribution, while the full genus-$g$ GW invariant receives additional contributions from higher BPS states $n_h^g$ with $g \leq h$. The identification of $F_g$ with the shadow free energy requires the full shadow tower of $A_{K3,\mathrm{rel}}$, whose higher-genus structure depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.
\end{enumerate}
 succeeded in 52ms:
\begin{itemize}
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. The automorphic correction of the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_X$ is identified with the shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_{A_X}$; the denominator identity equals the bar-complex Euler product.
 \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$): the weight of $\Delta_5$ is $5 = h^{1,1}(K3)/4 = 20/4$; this appears in the structural position of a modular characteristic, but without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), the identification $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.
 \item \textbf{Theorem (Shadow--Siegel gap)}: The shadow obstruction tower of $K3 \times E$ does not produce the Igusa cusp form $\Phi_{10}$. Four structural obstructions: categorical (number vs function), modular characteristic ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3 \neq 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$), second quantization (single-copy vs DMVV symmetric product), and Schottky at $g \geq 4$ ($\mathrm{codim} = (g{-}2)(g{-}3)/2$). Thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13) and ten research programmes (A through J) are developed in the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter.
\end{itemize}

\section{What is proved versus what is conjectural}
\label{sec:proved-vs-conjectural}

\noindent\textbf{Proved (would survive a referee):}
\begin{itemize}
 \item The generalized root datum axioms CY1--CY7, adequate for $K3 \times E$ and toric CY3 examples.
 \item The $E_2$-chiral algebra formalism and its basic properties (definition, bar complex, braiding).
 \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 2$: steps 1--3 of $\Phi$ (cyclic $\Ainf \to$ Lie conformal $\to$ factorization envelope $\to$ $E_2$ enhancement via $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing).
 \item The CoHA as $E_1$-sector for toric CY3 (via Schiffmann--Vasserot, RSYZ).
 \item The Drinfeld center equivalence $\cZ(\Rep^{E_1}(A)) \simeq \Rep^{E_2}(\Zder(A))$ (Ben-Zvi--Francis--Nadler, Lurie).
 \item All lattice theory and BKM constructions for $K3 \times E$ (Gritsenko--Nikulin).
 \item The denominator identity $\Delta_5 = \Phi$ and product formula (degree 2--6 computationally verified).
 succeeded in 50ms:

\begin{example}[Quintic threefold, $d = 3$]
\label{ex:quintic-derived}
The smooth quintic $X_5 \subset \P^4$ is a CY$_3$ with $h^{1,1} = 1$ and $h^{2,1} = 101$. Hochschild cohomology has
\[
 \HH^1 \simeq H^1(X_5, T_{X_5}) \;\text{of dimension } 101, \qquad
 \HH^2 \simeq H^2(X_5, T_{X_5}) \oplus H^1(X_5, \cO_{X_5}) = H^2(X_5, T_{X_5}).
\]
The CY$_3$ structure $S_{X_5} = [3]$ is the Serre functor used by Bridgeland in his construction of stability conditions. Theorem~CY-A at $d = 3$ is programme-level: $A_{X_5}$ is not constructed, and any downstream statement about it must carry \ClaimStatusConditional.
\end{example}

\begin{example}[CY$_4$ and SYZ fibrations]
\label{ex:cy4-syz}
For a CY $4$-fold $X$ admitting a Strominger--Yau--Zaslow special Lagrangian $T^4$-fibration $\pi \colon X \to B$, the dual fibration $\pi^\vee \colon X^\vee \to B$ is again a CY $4$-fold and mirror to $X$ (Strominger--Yau--Zaslow 1996; conjectural in general, known for hyperK\"ahler families). On the derived side, $D^b(\Coh(X))$ is CY$_4$ with $S_X = [4]$. The Vol~III programme for $d = 4$ is strictly conditional on CY-A$_3$ being resolved first.
\end{example}

\section{Homological mirror symmetry as chiral equivalence}
\label{sec:hms-chiral-derived}

Kontsevich's homological mirror symmetry conjecture (ICM 1994) predicts that mirror CY $d$-folds have equivalent derived categories, swapping the A-side and the B-side.
 succeeded in 50ms:
\end{remark}

\subsection{The missing vertical axis: Theorems~D and~H for the \texorpdfstring{$\Eone$}{E1} family}
\label{subsec:yangian-vertical-axis}

The Heisenberg frame chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:heisenberg-frame})
instantiates all five theorems on a single $\Einf$-chiral algebra.
The Yangian instantiates Theorems~A and~B
(Remark~\ref{rem:yangian-three-theorems}), and
Conjecture~\ref{conj:e1-genus-theory} formulates the $\Eone$ analog
of Theorem~C (complementarity on bordered moduli). Two theorems remain:
the modular characteristic (Theorem~D) and the Hochschild coefficient
ring (Theorem~H). Their $\Eone$ formulations require the following
structural replacements.

 succeeded in 51ms:
Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-cg-typeA}.
The multiplicity-free property is not contained in a single
reference: for simply-laced types it follows from
$(q,t)$-character theory~\cite{Nakajima04}; for classical types
from explicit formulas~\cite{ChariMoura06}; for all types the
Frenkel--Mukhin algorithm~\cite{FrenkelMukhin01} computes the
$q$-character and confirms multiplicity-freeness.
The block criterion transfers from quantum affine to Yangian
because it is purely combinatorial, depending only on Drinfeld
polynomial data identified by~\cite{GTL17}.
\end{remark}

 succeeded in 50ms:

\begin{theorem}[Koszul duality for Hochschild cohomology
\textup{(}Theorem~H\textup{)}; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{thm:main-koszul-hoch}
\index{Hochschild cohomology!Koszul duality|textbf}
\textup{[Regime: quadratic on the Koszul locus
\textup{(}Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:regime-tags})}.]}

For every chiral Koszul datum~$\cA$
\textup{(}Definition~\textup{\ref{def:chiral-koszul-morphism})} with
dual~$\cA^!$ on a smooth projective curve~$X$, the bigraded chiral
Hochschild complex
\textup{(}Definition~\textup{\ref{def:bigraded-hochschild})} satisfies
the \emph{derived-level} duality:
\begin{equation}\label{eq:hochschild-H-derived}
RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
\;\simeq\;
R\mathrm{Hom}\bigl(RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA^!),\, \omega_X[2]\bigr).
\end{equation}
On cohomology:
 succeeded in 50ms:
For a coweight $\mu \in X_*(\mathfrak{h})$ and simple $\fg$:
\begin{equation}\label{eq:shifted-langlands-dual}
Y_\mu(\fg)^! \;\simeq\; Y_{-\mu}(\fg^\vee)
\end{equation}
as $\Eone$-chiral algebras, where $\fg^\vee$ is the Langlands dual and
$-\mu$ is the negated coweight (viewed as a weight of $\fg^\vee$ via
the canonical identification $X_*(\mathfrak{h}) \cong X^*(\mathfrak{h}^\vee)$).
For unshifted $\mu = 0$, this reduces to the Yangian Koszul duality
$Y(\fg)^! \cong Y_{R^{-1}}(\fg)$
(Theorem~\ref{thm:yangian-koszul-dual}) when $\fg$ is simply-laced.
(Contributing to Conjecture~\ref{conj:master-dk-kl}.)
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Scope]
This is an $\Eone$-chiral incarnation of symplectic duality (Braden--Licata--Proudfoot--Webster \cite{BLPW16}): the shift $\mu \mapsto -\mu$ reverses monopole boundary conditions, i.e.\ $R(u) \mapsto R(u)^{-1}$ with Langlands involution. A proof requires Conjecture~\ref{conj:shifted-yangian-e1} and identification of the bar complex at coweight $\mu$ with the cobar at $-\mu$ for $\fg^\vee$.
\end{remark}

chapters/examples/quantum_group_reps.tex:499: the associated chiral algebra (Volume~I, Theorem~D);
chapters/connections/modular_koszul_bridge.tex:4:A CY category $\cC$ produces, via the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ of Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral}, a chiral algebra $A_\cC$; the bar complex $B(A_\cC) = T^c(s^{-1}\overline{A_\cC})$, built on the augmentation ideal $\overline{A_\cC} = \ker(\varepsilon)$, is a factorization coalgebra on $\Ran(C)$. Three Volume~I structures act on $B(A_\cC)$. The Verdier intertwining $D_{\Ran}(B(A)) \simeq B(A^!)$ of Theorem~A is a functor of factorization coalgebras on $\Ran(C)$; it is the Koszul duality, not bar-cobar inversion, and not the chiral derived center. Complementarity (Theorem~C) splits the genus-$g$ shadow complex into Verdier eigenspaces and, on the uniform-weight lane, equates the scalar sum of Koszul-dual modular characteristics to a family-dependent Koszul conductor. The genus tower (Theorem~D) identifies $\mathrm{obs}_g$ with $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} \cdot \lambda_g$ on the uniform-weight lane at genus $1$ unconditionally, with a cross-channel correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ at $g \geq 2$ for multi-weight algebras. Vol~III inherits three deficiencies. First, the convolution dg Lie algebra living on $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$ has no existing CY-side habitat. Second, the Vol~I scalar complementarity (Vol~I Theorem~C$_2$, with its family-dependent Koszul conductor; see Remark~\ref{rem:cy-complementarity-kappa-zero} below) has no CY translation stating which Koszul conductor $K_X$ applies at $d \in \{2, 3\}$. Third, the Vol~I CohFT promotion (Theorem~D$+$H) has no CY restatement tracking the flat identity axiom through $\Phi$. Five sections address these deficiencies and their consequences: \S\ref{sec:modular-conv-cy} builds the CY modular convolution algebra; \S\ref{sec:cy-complementarity-bridge} transports complementarity with explicit (C1) versus (C2) scoping and explicit $d = 2$ versus $d = 3$ conditionality; \S\ref{sec:cy-shadow-cohft} upgrades the shadow tower to a CohFT on $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$ and records how the Borcherds lift converts the $K3 \times E$ tower into the genus-$2$ Igusa cusp form $\Phi_{10}$; \S\ref{sec:hochschild-bridge} establishes the bridge between the three Hochschild theories (categorical, chiral, derived-center) through $\Phi$; and \S\ref{sec:cy-bridge-examples} collects the principal examples with their $\kappa_\bullet$-spectra.
chapters/connections/modular_koszul_bridge.tex:89:The free-field argument: the generating space of $A_\cC$ is $\HH^{\bullet+1}(\cC)$, and $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ equals the supertrace of the identity on this generating space, which is $\chi^{\CY}(\cC) = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC)$. The quantization step in the construction of $\Phi$ (CY-A, Step~4) preserves $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ at $d = 2$: the holomorphic anomaly cancellation at $d = 2$ (Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [2]$) guarantees that no quantum correction shifts the supertrace. The genus-$g$ obstruction formula is Vol~I Theorem~D applied to $A_\cC$; the substitution $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$ follows from the first part.

chapters/connections/modular_koszul_bridge.tex:209:Vol~I Theorem~D+H constructs the CohFT structure on the shadow tower of a chiral algebra with flat identity. at root of unity, and $\mathrm{KL}_k(\frakg)$ is the

\begin{remark}[Bar-complex interpretation]
\label{rem:kl-bar-complex}
From the bar-complex perspective, the KL equivalence states that
the $\Eone$-representation category of the affine vertex algebra
$V_k(\frakg)$ recovers the quantum group representation category.
The Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\frakg))$ encodes the quantum group
$R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component; the DK bridge (Volume~II,
MC3 for all simple types) extends this to the full categorical
equivalence.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Status of Conjecture CY-C]
\label{rem:cy-c-status}
Item (i) is constructed for $\frakg = \fsl_N$ via the Bridgeland
stability conditions on the CY$_2$ resolution of the $A_{N-1}$
surface singularity. Item (ii) at the $\Eone$ level is the content
of MC3 (proved on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types, Volume~I/II). The $\Etwo$
upgrade requires the Drinfeld center passage
(Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}). Item (iii) follows from
Theorem~CY-D (Theorem~\ref{thm:cy-modular-characteristic}) when
the CY category is constructed.
\end{remark}

\section{The modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$}
\label{sec:kappa-cat}

%%: kappa always subscripted in Vol III.
%%: kappa depends on the full algebra, not just the Virasoro subalgebra.

The modular characteristic of the quantum group representation
category connects the categorical datum to the genus expansion of
Volume~I.

\begin{proposition}[$\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$ for quantum groups]
\label{prop:kappa-cat-quantum-groups}
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
For the affine vertex algebra $V_k(\frakg)$ at level $k$ with
$q = e^{\pi i/(k + h^\vee)}$, the modular characteristic of
the associated CY$_2$ category $\cC(\frakg, q)$
(Conjecture~\ref{conj:qg-realization}) is:
\[
 \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC(\frakg, q))
 succeeded in 52ms:
% ============================================================

Quantum geometric Langlands (QGL), introduced by Feigin--Frenkel and developed by Frenkel--Gaitsgory (2006), is a one-parameter deformation of classical geometric Langlands. The parameter $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}$ is a complex number. The two limits recover known correspondences:
\begin{itemize}
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}} \to \infty$ (the critical level $k = -h^\vee$): the classical geometric Langlands correspondence of Beilinson--Drinfeld, with Feigin--Frenkel centers as the spectral side.
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}} \to 0$: the classical de Rham geometric Langlands in Gaitsgory's formulation.
\end{itemize}

At finite $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}$ the spectral side is a category of \emph{twisted D-modules} on $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ at level $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}$, and the automorphic side is the corresponding category at level $-1/\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}$ for the Langlands dual group. The quantum correspondence is stated as an equivalence of DG categories (Frenkel, \emph{Lectures on Quantum Geometric Langlands}, 2005; Gaitsgory's ongoing programme).

\paragraph{The identification conjecture.} The parameter $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}$ acts on the $\Etwo$-chiral algebra $V_{k}(\frakg)$ via $\kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}} = k + h^\vee$. Volume~I defines a distinct parameter $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$, the chiral modular characteristic (Volume~I, Theorem~D), which for $V_k(\frakg)$ evaluates to $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \dim(\frakg)(k + h^\vee)/(2 h^\vee)$. The two are proportional:
\[
 \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(V_k(\frakg)) \;=\; \frac{\dim(\frakg)}{2 h^\vee} \cdot \kappa_{\mathrm{QGL}}.
\]
At the critical level both vanish. This proportionality is already unconditional for $V_k(\frakg)$; the conjectural extension is to general CY input.

\begin{conjecture}[QGL parameter equals shadow obstruction; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
\label{conj:qgl-equals-shadow}
For every CY category $\cC$ of dimension $d = 2$, the quantum geometric Langlands parameter of the Frenkel--Gaitsgory equivalence applied to $\Phi(\cC)$ coincides, up to the dimensional normalization $\dim(\frakg)/(2 h^\vee)$ in the Kac--Moody case, with the Vol~I chiral shadow parameter $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ of the CY $2$-category. Equivalently, the Frenkel--Gaitsgory QGL deformation is the shadow obstruction tower of Vol~I (Volume~I, Theorem~D) evaluated along the critical-level family.
\end{conjecture}
 succeeded in 52ms:
\begin{proposition}[Complementarity for $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$]
\label{prop:kappa-cat-complementarity}
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
Under Koszul duality $V_k(\frakg) \mapsto V_{k'}(\frakg)$ with
$k' = -k - 2h^\vee$ (Feigin--Frenkel involution):
\[
 \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC(\frakg, q))
 + \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC(\frakg, q'))
 = 0
\]
where $q' = e^{\pi i/(k' + h^\vee)} = e^{-\pi i/(k + h^\vee)}$.
This is the CY incarnation of the Volume~I complementarity
$\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}' = 0$ for KM/free fields (Volume~I, Theorem~C).
%: complementarity sum is NOT universally zero.
% For KM/free fields: kappa + kappa' = 0 (proved).
% For W-algebras: kappa + kappa' = rho*K (Theorem D).
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Under $k \mapsto k' = -k - 2h^\vee$:
 succeeded in 52ms:
presentation: $R_{12}(u-v) \, T_1(u) \, T_2(v)
= T_2(v) \, T_1(u) \, R_{12}(u-v)$ where $T(u) = \sum_r T_r u^{-r}$
is the generating matrix and $R_{12}(u)$ is the Yang $R$-matrix.
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[Yangian from collision residue]
\label{prop:yangian-collision}
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
The Yangian $Y(\frakg)$ is recovered from the degree-$(1,1)$ collision
residue of the ordered bar complex:
\[
 Y(\frakg) = \langle r^{\mathrm{coll}}(z) \rangle_{\mathrm{RTT}}
\]
where the RTT relation is the degree-$(1,1,1)$ bar differential.
This is the content of Volume~II, MC3: the ordered bar complex
$B^{\mathrm{ord}}(V_k(\frakg))$ produces the Yangian as the
universal algebra generated by the modes of
$r(z) = \frac{k\,\Omega}{z}$
.
\end{proposition}

/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/rectification_20260412_233715/R22_bar_construction.md:236:chapters/theory/bar_construction.tex:1401:Bar degree~$n$ uses sections over $C_{n+1}(X)$; the bar differential lowers bar degree. The \emph{reduced} bar complex, used from \S\ref{subsec:bar-functoriality} onward and throughout the monograph, replaces $\mathcal{A}$ by its augmentation ideal $\bar{\mathcal{A}} = \ker(\varepsilon \colon \mathcal{A} \to \omega_X)$ and starts at $\bar{B}^0 = \mathbb{C}$. Augmentation separates generators from scalars: the map~$\varepsilon$ extracts the component of each operator proportional to the identity (the vacuum expectation value), and the reduced complex $T^c(s^{-1}\bar{\mathcal{A}})$ tensors together only the non-scalar modes. Without this splitting, the reduced coproduct on the bar coalgebra would fail conilpotence, and the cofree coalgebra structure that drives the bar-cobar adjunction (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-adjunction}) would be unavailable. Every theorem stated later in this chapter refers to the reduced complex $T^c(s^{-1}\bar{\mathcal{A}})$; the unreduced display above has no independent content.

/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/fix_wave_A_20260413_112727/A13_fix_hardcoded_parts_v1.md:453:/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/wave2_audit_20260413_001942/D09_five_objects_discipline.md:255:CENTER	/Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups/chapters/connections/cy_holographic_datum_master.tex	926	\begin{remark}[The three seven-face masters] \label{rem:three-seven-face-masters} The three volumes each devote a part to the seven-face programme, with the same architecture but different ground objects: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\arabic*)] \item \emph{Vol~I, Part~III:} the binary collision residue of a chiral algebra on a curve, in seven languages: bar-cobar twisting, primitive generator, classical $r$-matrix, KZ connection, Gaudin model, Bethe ansatz, dg-shifted Yangian (cf.\ Vol~I, Theorem~\ref{thm:vol1-seven-face-master}). \item \emph{Vol~II, Part~III:} the binary collision residue of a holomorphic-topological quantum group, in seven languages: open-string brace algebra, derived center, twisted holography boundary, line defect, Wilson line $R$-matrix, soft graviton coupling, Yangian double (cf.\ Vol~II, Theorem~\ref{thm:vol2-seven-face-master}). \item \emph{Vol~III, this chapter:} the binary CY collision residue of a Calabi--Yau chiral algebra, in seven CY-specific languages: CY bar-cobar, CoHA / perverse coherent sheaves, classical CY Poisson coisson, MO stable envelope, affine super Yangian for toric CY$_3$, elliptic Sklyanin for toroidal CY, Gaudin from CY$_3$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:cy-seven-face-master} above). \end{enumerate} The three master theorems are mutually compatible: under the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$, face~$i$ of Vol~III maps to a specialization of face~$i$ of Vol~I, and similarly for Vol~II. The CY setting is the most constrained: each face acquires geometric content that the abstract chiral algebra setting does not see (DT counts, plane partitions, crystal melting, MO stable envelopes). \end{remark}

Frobenius structure at the chain level. The functor $\Phi$
converts this datum into a chiral algebra $A_\cC$ on~$X$ in
three steps: the cyclic $\Ainf$-structure determines a Lie
conformal algebra~$L_\cC$; the factorization envelope
$U_X^{\mathrm{fact}}(L_\cC)$ produces a chiral algebra
$A_\cC$; the $\Sd$-framing enhances the factorization
structure. For $d = 2$, the $\mathbb{S}^2$-action on
Hochschild homology gives an $\Etwo$-chiral algebra with
braided monoidal representation category, and all three steps
are proved (Theorem~CY-A). For $d = 3$, holomorphic
Chern--Simons breaks $\Etwo$ to~$\Eone$; the braiding is
recovered via the Drinfeld center of the $\Eone$-monoidal
representation category. Steps~(1) and~(2) are unconditional;
step~(3) is a programme conditional on the chain-level
$\mathbb{S}^3$-framing.

algebraic-geometric framework of Volume~I to concrete
enumerative geometry.

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Maturity and scope.}
This volume is the least mature of the three. The
CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ is proved for $d = 2$
(Theorem~CY-A) and is a programme for $d = 3$, conditional on
the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing. The quantum group
realization (CY-C) is conjectural: the object $C(\frakg,q)$ is
not constructed in general. The modular CY characteristic
(CY-D) is well-defined only when $A_\cC$ exists. What is
proved is stated as proved; what is conjectural is stated as
conjecture; what is a programme is described as such.

\chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
\label{ch:cyclic-ainf}

A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.

\section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
\label{sec:ainf-algebras}

\begin{definition}[$\Ainf$-algebra]
\label{def:ainf-algebra}
An \emph{$\Ainf$-algebra} over a field $k$ is a $\Z$-graded $k$-vector space $A$ equipped with operations
\[

\[
 r_{CY}^{(\mathrm{F}_i)} \;=\;
 \Phi_{i,j}\bigl(r_{CY}^{(\mathrm{F}_j)}\bigr)
 \qquad \forall\, i, j \in \{1, \ldots, 7\},
\]
for canonical change-of-language morphisms $\Phi_{i,j}$ given explicitly
by Theorem~CY-A (face~1 to face~3), the
Schiffmann--Vasserot/RSYZ identification (face~2 to face~5), the MO
stable envelope (face~4), the elliptic specialization $\hbar \to 0$
(face~6 to face~3), and the Litvinov Bethe ansatz (face~5 to face~7).
The limits between faces are recorded in the seven-face commuting
diagram (cf.\ Vol~I,
Theorem~\ref{thm:vol1-seven-face-master}; Vol~II,
Theorem~\ref{thm:vol2-seven-face-master}).
\end{conjecture}

%% ===================================================================
%% Closing cross-reference
%% ===================================================================

The five sections transport the Vol~I modular Koszul machine into the CY geometric realm: the convolution algebra of \S\ref{sec:modular-conv-cy} is the working surface, the complementarity of \S\ref{sec:cy-complementarity-bridge} is the duality statement, the CohFT of \S\ref{sec:cy-shadow-cohft} is the genus tower, the Hochschild bridge of \S\ref{sec:hochschild-bridge} identifies which Hochschild theory controls which invariant, and the examples of \S\ref{sec:cy-bridge-examples} verify the $\kappa_\bullet$-spectrum against independent computations. The $d = 2$ case is unconditional (CY-A proved, Theorem~\ref{thm:cy-complementarity-d2}); the $d = 3$ case is the Vol~III programme (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-complementarity-d3}, Conjecture~\ref{conj:toric-cy3-shadow-cohft}, Conjecture~\ref{conj:hochschild-bridge-d3}). Verification of every $\kappa_\bullet$-value uses the independent paths of compute/lib/modular\_cy\_characteristic.py and compute/lib/cy\_euler.py, cross-checked against the $\kappa_\bullet$-spectrum $\{\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}, \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}, \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}, \kappa_{\mathrm{fiber}}\} = \{2, 3, 5, 24\}$ for $K3 \times E$.
 succeeded in 51ms:

\noindent\textit{Verification}: 119 tests in \texttt{cy3\_grand\_atlas.py}.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
The BCOV formula $F_1 = -\tfrac{1}{2} \log \det(\bar{\partial}^\dagger \bar{\partial})$ on a compact CY$_3$ $X$ gives $F_1 = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}(X)/24$ when $h^{1,0}(X) = 0$. The condition $h^{1,0} = 0$ ensures that the genus-$1$ free energy has no contribution from abelian zero modes; the formula holds for the quintic ($h^{2,1} = 101$) and all other CICYs with $h^{1,0} = 0$, regardless of the number of complex structure moduli.

For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of BPS bound states across fibers contributes additional genus-$1$ data beyond the one-loop determinant. For $K3 \times E$: the $24$ free bosons from the K3 fiber give $\kappa_{\mathrm{fiber}} = 24$, but the sewing along $E$ via the DMVV formula introduces imaginary root contributions that produce $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5) = 5$ (the weight of the Igusa cusp form). The ``lost'' $19$ units ($24 - 5 = 19$) are absorbed by the Borcherds product structure.

For the resolved conifold: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 2$ (the total space deformation retracts onto $\bP^1$) gives $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = 1/12$, but the DT computation gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ (the conifold has a single BPS state contributing at genus~$1$).
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
\label{prop:categorical-euler}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).

The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.

Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 succeeded in 51ms:
\chapter{The $K3 \times E$ Tower and the Igusa Cusp Form}
\label{ch:k3-times-e}

The threefold $K3 \times E$ is a fibration of a CY$_2$ over a CY$_1$. Does its chiral algebra decompose accordingly? A naive Fubini argument would predict $A_{K3 \times E} \simeq A_{K3} \otimes A_E$, and the modular characteristic would split additively as $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1 = 3$. But the Oberdieck--Pixton DT partition function of $K3 \times E$ is $C / (\Delta_5)^2$, where $\Delta_5$ is the Gritsenko--Nikulin automorphic form of weight~$5$ on $\mathrm{O}^+(3,2)$. The weight $5$ does not match the sum $3$: $5 \neq 2 + 1$.

Two different modular characteristics are in play, and conflating them is the source of the subscripted-$\kappa_{\bullet}$ confusion that was introduced to prevent. The chiral de Rham complex gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = 3 = \dim_\C$, honestly additive over the fibration. The Borcherds lift weight gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$, which is not a modular characteristic of any constructed chiral algebra: it is a weight attached to a generalized Borcherds--Kac--Moody superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ through its denominator identity. The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ of a putative CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not constructed.

This chapter treats $K3 \times E$ as the prototype for the $d = 3$ programme. The concrete object of study is the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ attached to $X = (S \times E)/(\mathbb{Z}/N\mathbb{Z})$, together with the Oberdieck--Pixton theorem identifying its denominator with the Igusa cusp form. The goal is to understand how much of the Vol~I bar-cobar apparatus survives in the $d = 3$ regime, where the CY-to-chiral functor is conjectural: which identities among root multiplicities, genus-$g$ partition functions, and lattice theta series are genuinely theorems versus conjectural identifications awaiting the $d = 3$ functor. The chapter concludes with the K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$ (Definition~\ref{def:k3-double-current-algebra}), the K3 analogue of the double current algebra $\fg \otimes \bC[u,v]$ in which the polynomial ring is replaced by $H^*(S,\bC)$ and the polynomial residue pairing by the Mukai pairing; the resulting finite-dimensional Lie algebra serves as the classical limit of the conjectural ``K3 Yangian'' whose quantization is governed by the Maulik--Okounkov $R$-matrix (Theorem~\ref{thm:k3e-mo-rmatrix}).

\section{The CY3 geometry}
\label{sec:k3e-geometry}

Consider the rank-4 free $\mathbb{Z}$-module $\Lambda^4 = \mathbb{Z}e_1 \oplus \mathbb{Z}e_2 \oplus \mathbb{Z}e_3 \oplus \mathbb{Z}e_4$. The exterior square $\bigwedge^2 \colon \mathrm{SL}_4(\mathbb{Z}) \to \mathrm{GL}(\bigwedge^2 \Lambda^4)$ and the Pfaffian scalar product $(u,v) = u \wedge v / (e_1 \wedge e_2 \wedge e_3 \wedge e_4)$ give a signature $(3,3)$ form. The lattice
\[
 \Lambda^{3,2} = (e_1 \wedge e_3 + e_2 \wedge e_4)^\perp \subset \bigwedge^2 \Lambda^4
\]
has signature $(3,2)$ and satisfies $\Lambda^{3,2} \simeq \Lambda^{1,1} \oplus \Lambda^{1,1} \oplus [2]$.

\begin{lemma}
\label{lem:sp4-so-iso}
The correspondence $\bigwedge^2$ defines an isomorphism
\[
 % B-class fix: Sp_4 is 4x4, so quotient by +-I_4; O(Lambda^{3,2}) is
 % 5x5, so quotient by +-I_5.
 \bigwedge^2 \colon \mathrm{Sp}_4(\mathbb{Z})/\{\pm I_4\} \xrightarrow{\;\sim\;} \mathrm{O}(\Lambda^{3,2})_+/\{\pm I_5\}.
\]

 succeeded in 51ms:
This is the holomorphic Euler characteristic of $\cC$ as a CY category (equivalently, for $\cC = D^b(\Coh(X))$ with $X$ a smooth projective CY$_d$ manifold, $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC) = \chi(\cO_X) = \sum_i (-1)^i h^{0,i}(X)$). It is distinct \emph{a priori} from the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC)$ of the chiral algebra $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$; the two coincide at $d = 2$ (Proposition~\ref{prop:kappa-cat-chi-cy}) and are conjectured to coincide at $d \geq 3$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-kappa-identification}; see Remark~\ref{rem:kappa-cat-vs-kappa-ch} below).
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[$\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ under $\Phi$ at $d = 2$]
\label{prop:kappa-cat-chi-cy}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere
For $\cC$ a smooth proper CY$_2$ category with chiral algebra $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ (CY-A at $d = 2$, PROVED),
\[
 \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC) \;=\; \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC) \;=\; \chi^{\CY}(\cC).
\]
Consequently, for every genus $g \geq 1$ and on the uniform-weight lane,
\[
 \mathrm{obs}_g(A_\cC) \;=\; \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC) \cdot \lambda_g
 \qquad (g \geq 1,\;\textup{UNIFORM-WEIGHT});
\]
at genus $g \geq 2$ for multi-weight algebras the scalar formula receives a nonvanishing cross-channel correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$ (ALL-WEIGHT, with cross-channel correction $\delta F_g^{\mathrm{cross}}$).
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
The free-field argument: the generating space of $A_\cC$ is $\HH^{\bullet+1}(\cC)$, and $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ equals the supertrace of the identity on this generating space, which is $\chi^{\CY}(\cC) = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cC)$. The quantization step in the construction of $\Phi$ (CY-A, Step~4) preserves $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ at $d = 2$: the holomorphic anomaly cancellation at $d = 2$ (Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [2]$) guarantees that no quantum correction shifts the supertrace. The genus-$g$ obstruction formula is Vol~I Theorem~D applied to $A_\cC$; the substitution $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$ follows from the first part.

chapters/theory/introduction.tex:267:\emph{The $K3 \times E$ tower.} For $X = (S \times E)/(\mathbb{Z}/N\mathbb{Z})$ with $S$ a K3 surface and $E$ an elliptic curve, the lattice is $\Lambda^{3,2} \simeq \Lambda^{1,1} \oplus \Lambda^{1,1} \oplus [2]$ of signature $(3,2)$. The hyperbolic sublattice $\Lambda^{2,1}_{II}$ with Gram matrix $\bigl(\begin{smallmatrix} 2 & -2 & -2 \\ -2 & 2 & -2 \\ -2 & -2 & 2 \end{smallmatrix}\bigr)$ provides the real roots. The root multiplicities are the Fourier coefficients $f(n,l)$ of the weak Jacobi form $\phi_{0,1}$, the K3 elliptic genus. The resulting generalized Borcherds--Kac--Moody superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ has the Igusa cusp form $\Delta_5$ as its denominator identity. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic satisfies $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = 3 = \dim_\C$ (from additivity: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), verified by six independent paths; the BKM automorphic weight is the distinct quantity $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ (the weight of $\Delta_5$; see the Shadow--Siegel gap theorem below); the factor~$2$ in $Z_{K3} = 2\,\phi_{0,1}$ is the bar-complex moonshine multiplier $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(\cA_{K3})$; and the shadow obstruction tower does not produce $\Delta_5$ directly (four structural obstructions: categorical, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$-mismatch, second quantization, Schottky; Theorem~\ref{thm:shadow-siegel-gap}).

chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:1:\chapter{The $K3 \times E$ Tower and the Igusa Cusp Form}
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:4:The threefold $K3 \times E$ is a fibration of a CY$_2$ over a CY$_1$. Does its chiral algebra decompose accordingly? A naive Fubini argument would predict $A_{K3 \times E} \simeq A_{K3} \otimes A_E$, and the modular characteristic would split additively as $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1 = 3$. But the Oberdieck--Pixton DT partition function of $K3 \times E$ is $C / (\Delta_5)^2$, where $\Delta_5$ is the Gritsenko--Nikulin automorphic form of weight~$5$ on $\mathrm{O}^+(3,2)$. The weight $5$ does not match the sum $3$: $5 \neq 2 + 1$.
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:6:Two different modular characteristics are in play, and conflating them is the source of the subscripted-$\kappa_{\bullet}$ confusion that was introduced to prevent. The chiral de Rham complex gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = 3 = \dim_\C$, honestly additive over the fibration. The Borcherds lift weight gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$, which is not a modular characteristic of any constructed chiral algebra: it is a weight attached to a generalized Borcherds--Kac--Moody superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ through its denominator identity. The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ of a putative CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not constructed.
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:8:This chapter treats $K3 \times E$ as the prototype for the $d = 3$ programme. The concrete object of study is the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ attached to $X = (S \times E)/(\mathbb{Z}/N\mathbb{Z})$, together with the Oberdieck--Pixton theorem identifying its denominator with the Igusa cusp form. The goal is to understand how much of the Vol~I bar-cobar apparatus survives in the $d = 3$ regime, where the CY-to-chiral functor is conjectural: which identities among root multiplicities, genus-$g$ partition functions, and lattice theta series are genuinely theorems versus conjectural identifications awaiting the $d = 3$ functor. The chapter concludes with the K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$ (Definition~\ref{def:k3-double-current-algebra}), the K3 analogue of the double current algebra $\fg \otimes \bC[u,v]$ in which the polynomial ring is replaced by $H^*(S,\bC)$ and the polynomial residue pairing by the Mukai pairing; the resulting finite-dimensional Lie algebra serves as the classical limit of the conjectural ``K3 Yangian'' whose quantization is governed by the Maulik--Okounkov $R$-matrix (Theorem~\ref{thm:k3e-mo-rmatrix}).
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:158:\section{The quantum vertex chiral group $G(K3 \times E)$}
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:164: \item \textbf{(Theorem.)} The generalized root datum $\mathcal{R}(K3 \times E)$ is $(\Lambda^{3,2}, \Delta^{\mathrm{re}}, \Delta^{\mathrm{im}}_0, \Delta^{\mathrm{im}}_1, W^{(2)}(\Lambda^{2,1}_{II}), \rho, f(nm,l))$. This is a mathematical fact (Gritsenko--Nikulin).
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:165: \item \textbf{(Conjecture.)} There exists a chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X)))$ whose bar complex $B(A)$ encodes the product formula for $\Delta_5$. \emph{Note}: the functor $\Phi$ is constructed for $d = 2$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$); the $d = 3$ case (which applies here, since $K3 \times E$ is CY$_3$) is the content of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$.
chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:166: \item \textbf{(Observation/Conjecture.)} The number $5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5) = h^{1,1}(K3)/4$ appears in the structural position of a modular characteristic: $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$. Without the chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$, this identification is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.

chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:279:At rank $0$ (curve class $\beta = 0$), the DT theory of $K3 \times E$ reduces to the MacMahon function weighted by $q^{1/24} \prod_{n \geq 1}(1 - q^n)^{-24}$, i.e.\ the reciprocal of $\eta(q)^{24} / q$. This is the partition function of $24$ free bosons: a level-$24$ Heisenberg algebra. The rank-$0$ sector is thus controlled by $\cH_{24}$, with $\kappa_{\mathrm{fiber}}(\cH_{24}) = 24$.

chapters/examples/k3_times_e.tex:376:(ii) The lattice $\Lambda_{K3}$ has rank $22$ with signature $(3,19)$. The sublattice $\Lambda^{3,2}$ retains $5$ of the $22$ directions; the kernel has rank $22 - 5 = 17$. Including the $2$ directions from the elliptic curve $E$ (giving the full Mukai lattice of $K3 \times E$ of rank~$24$), the lattice-theoretic deficit is $24 - 5 = 19$. The imaginary root multiplicities of $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ are determined by $c(D)$ for $D > 0$; the dimension count $19$ matches the codimension of $\Lambda^{3,2}$ in the Mukai lattice, but the precise correspondence between lattice directions and imaginary root families remains to be established.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2000,2042p' chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 51ms:
\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
\label{prop:categorical-euler}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).

The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.

Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
 \item Weight of the Igusa cusp form: $\mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5) = 5$.
 \item DMVV exponent: the Borcherds lift weight formula gives $c(0)/2 = 10/2 = 5$.
 \item Bar Euler product: the genus-$1$ coefficient of $\log Z_{\DT}$ gives $F_1 = 5/24$.
 \item Relative DT: fiber-by-fiber computation via K3 fibers sewed along $E$.
 \item Anomaly cancellation: the worldsheet anomaly for the $K3 \times E$ sigma model.
\end{enumerate}

\noindent\textit{Verification}: 78 tests in \texttt{k3e\_relative\_shadow.py}; 119 tests in \texttt{cy3\_grand\_atlas.py}.
\end{proposition}

chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1605:Let $\cC$ be a CY$_3$ category satisfying hypotheses \ref{hyp:smooth}--\ref{hyp:framing} of Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}}, with CY-to-chiral algebra $A_\cC = \Phi_3(\cC)$ and modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC) = \chi^{\CY}(\cC)$ (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}).

chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1723:The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The shadow data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)/1$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The genus-$1$ shadow $F_1 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$ matches the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.
chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1770:\noindent Three remarks on the table entries. First, $K3 \times E$ does not have a quiver atlas in the strict sense of Definition~\ref{def:quiver-chart-atlas}: the derived category $D^b(\Coh(K3 \times E))$ does not admit a single tilting generator, and the fibration structure requires a different gluing mechanism (the relative Fourier--Mukai, see Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The table records $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathrm{K3}) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$, Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}); the distinct Borcherds automorphic weight is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$. Second, the quintic has $|I| = 2$ charts: one at large volume (a quiver chart from the Beilinson collection restricted to $X$) and one at the Gepner point (a matrix factorization category $\MF(W_{\mathrm{Fermat}})$, which is NOT a quiver chart; see Remark~\ref{rem:gepner-lv-dichotomy}). Third, the shadow class and depth $r_{\mathrm{max}}$ refer to the Heisenberg truncation ($s = 1$ channel). At the full spin tower, the classification may differ (Remark~\ref{rem:c3-per-channel}).

\begin{conjecture}[CY modular characteristic at $d \geq 3$]
\label{conj:cy-kappa-identification}
\ClaimStatusConjectured{}
For $\cC$ a smooth proper CY category of dimension $d \geq 3$, assuming the $d$-dimensional CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ exists (CY-A$_d$, PROGRAMME), the chiral algebra $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ satisfies
\[
 \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC) \;=\; \chi^{\CY}(\cC).
\]
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Evidence for the $d \geq 3$ conjecture]
\label{rem:cy-kappa-evidence}
At $d = 2$, the identification is proved (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:cy-kappa-d2}}). At $d = 3$, the conjecture is verified for $\C^3$ where both sides are independently computable ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} = 1$; see Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}). The obstruction to extending the $d = 2$ proof is that the $d = 3$ quantization step may introduce corrections: Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [3]$ no longer kills the one-loop anomaly by parity. The $d = 3$ functor itself is conjectural beyond $\C^3$.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Enriques surface: orbifold and kappa halving]
\label{rem:enriques-orbifold-kappa}
\ClaimStatusHeuristic{}
An Enriques surface $S$ is not a strict $\CY_2$ input in the sense of Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:cy-to-chiral}}, because $\omega_S$ is nontrivial $2$-torsion and the Serre functor on $D^b(\Coh(S))$ is $(-)\otimes \omega_S[2]$, not $[2]$. It is still the first orbifold test case next to the K3 example of Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:cy-kappa-evidence}}. Let $\pi \colon X \to S$ be the universal cover, where $X$ is a K3 surface and the deck involution acts freely. Then $\chi(\cO_X) = 2$ and $\chi(\cO_S) = 1$, so the K3 value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 2$ suggests the orbifold value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ if $\Phi$ extends to this torsion-canonical quotient surface.

\begin{conjecture}[Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-spectrum]
\label{conj:enriques-kappa-spectrum}
\ClaimStatusConjectured{}
Let $S$ be an Enriques surface, $X$ its K3 universal cover, $E$ an elliptic curve, and assume Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:phi-enriques-z2-orbifold}} and the extension of $\Phi$ to generalized CY$_3$ inputs. Then the three modular characteristics satisfy:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) = 1 = \chi(\cO_S)$, half the K3 value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(X) = 2$.
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 4$, the weight of the Allcock Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$. This is verified computationally by \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py} (72 tests; see Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:enriques-kappa-anomaly}} in the bar-cobar bridge chapter).
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By additivity (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}), $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
\end{enumerate}
The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[BKM lattice structure: Enriques vs.\ K3]
\label{rem:enriques-bkm-lattice}

This is the strongest form: the full coalgebra structure of the bar complex, not merely the Euler characteristic, matches the motivic Hall algebra of Kontsevich--Soibelman. The BPS invariants are recovered as $\Omega_{\mathrm{sh}}(n) = \dim H^1(B^{\Eone})_n = \Omega_{\DT}(n)$.

\end{enumerate}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Scope.}
Level~\ref{level:genus1} is proved for $\C^3$ (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:kappa-c3}}) and verified for $K3 \times E$ (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}). Level~\ref{level:virtual} is conditional on the uniform-weight hypothesis and on the existence of the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi_3$ (Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}}). Level~\ref{level:motivic} is conjectural in general; it is proved for toric CY$_3$ where the $\CoHA$ provides the motivic comparison.

The conjecture does NOT claim that the naive numerical DT invariants (e.g., the Fourier coefficients of $\Delta_5$ for $K3 \times E$) are recovered by the scalar shadow $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ alone. The higher Fourier coefficients encode information from the full bar complex (all degrees), not merely the leading shadow coefficient. The scalar shadow captures the leading asymptotics; the refined shadow (bar cohomology at all degrees) captures the full DT data.
\end{conjecture}


\begin{remark}[Level of validity and the three regimes]
\label{rem:dt-shadow-level}

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '120,138p' chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 52ms:
Three lines of evidence support the conjecture.
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\arabic*)}]
 \item \emph{Derived category descent.} The functor $\pi^* \colon D^b(\Coh(S)) \hookrightarrow D^b(\Coh(X))$ identifies $D^b(\Coh(S))$ with the $\iota^*$-invariant subcategory of $D^b(\Coh(X))$. (For a free action, this is the content of equivariant descent; see Bridgeland--King--Reid for the general framework.) If $\Phi$ commutes with taking $G$-invariants, item~(i) follows.
 \item \emph{Hochschild homology halving.} The Hochschild homology of the Enriques surface satisfies $\dim \HH_0(S) = 1$, $\dim \HH_1(S) = 0$, $\dim \HH_2(S) = 0$, giving $\chi^{\CY}(S) = 1 - 0 + 0 = 1$. (Here $\HH_2(S) = H^0(\omega_S) = 0$ because $\omega_S$ is nontrivial $2$-torsion; the torsion in $H^1(S, \bZ) = \bZ/2$ does not contribute to Hochschild homology over $\bC$.) This is half the K3 value $\chi^{\CY}(X) = 1 - 0 + 1 = 2$, consistent with the halving principle $\chi^{\CY}(S) = \chi^{\CY}(X)/|G|$ for the free $\bZ/2$-quotient. The precise relationship is: $\HH_\bullet(S) \simeq \HH_\bullet(X)^{\bZ/2}$ as graded vector spaces, where $\iota^*$ acts as $+1$ on $\HH_0(X) = H^0(\cO_X)$ and as $-1$ on $\HH_2(X) = H^0(\omega_X)$ (since $\iota^* \omega_X = -\omega_X$), so the invariant piece $\HH_2(X)^{\bZ/2} = 0$ recovers $\HH_2(S) = 0$.
 \item \emph{Chiral de Rham.} The chiral de Rham complex $\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}$ is defined on any smooth variety. For the Enriques surface, $H^*(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(S)) \simeq H^*(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(X))^{\bZ/2}$ by functoriality of the chiral de Rham sheaf under \'etale morphisms. This gives the chiral-algebra-level analogue of item~(i) at the level of vertex algebra cohomology.
\end{enumerate}
The principal obstruction is Step~4 of the cyclic-to-chiral passage (Section~\ref{sec:cyclic-to-chiral}): the CY trace on $D^b(\Coh(S))$ is $2$-torsion, so the quantization requires a $\bZ/2$-equivariant refinement.
\end{remark}

\begin{conjecture}[Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-spectrum]
\label{conj:enriques-kappa-spectrum}
\ClaimStatusConjectured{}
Let $S$ be an Enriques surface, $X$ its K3 universal cover, $E$ an elliptic curve, and assume Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:phi-enriques-z2-orbifold}} and the extension of $\Phi$ to generalized CY$_3$ inputs. Then the three modular characteristics satisfy:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) = 1 = \chi(\cO_S)$, half the K3 value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(X) = 2$.
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 4$, the weight of the Allcock Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$. This is verified computationally by \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py} (72 tests; see Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:enriques-kappa-anomaly}} in the bar-cobar bridge chapter).
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By additivity (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}), $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
\end{enumerate}
The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.

\begin{definition}[Binary collision residue, CY version]
\label{def:cy-collision-residue}
Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper Calabi--Yau category of dimension~$d$ with
holomorphic volume form $\Omega_\cC$. Let $A_\cC$ denote the chiral
algebra produced by the CY-to-chiral functor (Theorem~CY-A$_d$, proved
for $d = 2$, conditional on chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing for
$d = 3$). Let $\Theta_{A_\cC} \in \mathrm{MC}(\fg^{\mathrm{mod}}_{A_\cC})$
denote its universal Maurer--Cartan element (cf.\ Vol~I,
Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-adjunction}). The \emph{binary CY collision
residue} is
\[
 r_{CY}(z) \;:=\; \Res^{\mathrm{coll}}_{0,2}\bigl(\Theta_{A_\cC}\bigr)
 \;\in\; A_\cC \otimes A_\cC \,[\![z^{-1}]\!],
\]
the genus-$0$, degree-$2$ residue along the collision divisor of
 succeeded in 51ms:
The bar Euler product of lattice VOAs recovers the Borcherds denominator identity (verified for $E_8$ and Leech lattice; conditional on CY-A$_3$ for $K3 \times E$).

\section{Main results}
\label{sec:main-results}

\begin{itemize}
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. The automorphic correction of the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_X$ is identified with the shadow obstruction tower $\Theta_{A_X}$; the denominator identity equals the bar-complex Euler product.
 \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
 \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$): the weight of $\Delta_5$ is $5 = h^{1,1}(K3)/4 = 20/4$; this appears in the structural position of a modular characteristic, but without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), the identification $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.
 \item \textbf{Theorem (Shadow--Siegel gap)}: The shadow obstruction tower of $K3 \times E$ does not produce the Igusa cusp form $\Phi_{10}$. Four structural obstructions: categorical (number vs function), modular characteristic ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3 \neq 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$), second quantization (single-copy vs DMVV symmetric product), and Schottky at $g \geq 4$ ($\mathrm{codim} = (g{-}2)(g{-}3)/2$). Thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13) and ten research programmes (A through J) are developed in the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter.
\end{itemize}

\section{What is proved versus what is conjectural}
\label{sec:proved-vs-conjectural}

\noindent\textbf{Proved (would survive a referee):}
\begin{itemize}
 \item The generalized root datum axioms CY1--CY7, adequate for $K3 \times E$ and toric CY3 examples.
 \item The $E_2$-chiral algebra formalism and its basic properties (definition, bar complex, braiding).
 succeeded in 51ms:

 $\{\mu_k\}_{k \geq 1}$ and Poincar\'{e} pairing give the input

\begin{remark}[Well-definedness for general CY$_3$]
\label{rem:fact-coprod-general-cy3}
The factorization-homology coproduct $\Delta^{\mathrm{Ran}}$ is
well-defined for \emph{any} CY$_3$ category $\cC$ for which the
CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ produces a factorization algebra---i.e.,
whenever CY-A holds.
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
  \item \emph{At $d = 2$ (unconditional):} For a CY$_2$ category
  (e.g.\ $D^b(\Coh(K3))$), the functor $\Phi$ produces an
  $\Etwo$-chiral algebra (Theorem~CY-A at $d = 2$), and the
  factorization-homology coproduct is the excision map for any
  collar-gluing of the underlying surface.  This recovers the
  lattice vertex algebra coproduct (primitive on generators)
  without any toric structure.
