 succeeded in 51ms:
\end{table}

\begin{remark}[Detailed evidence for Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:shadow-bps-dt}}]
\label{rem:shadow-bps-evidence-detail}
The four cases in Table~\ref{tab:shadow-bps-evidence} have different logical dependencies.

\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

\item \textbf{$\C^3$: proved at the motivic level.}
The CY-to-chiral functor produces $A_{\C^3} = \cW_{1+\infty} = H_1$ (at the self-dual point). The bar Euler product is $1/M(q) = \prod (1-q^n)^n$ (Proposition~\ref{prop:c3-bar-euler}), inverting the MacMahon function. The bar cohomology gives $\Omega(n) = n = \Omega_{\DT}(n)$ at all degrees ($115$ tests). The motivic comparison holds: $B^{\Eone}(H_1)$ as a graded $\Eone$-coalgebra matches the motivic DT coalgebra of $\C^3$ via the Schiffmann--Vasserot identification $\CoHA(\C^3) = Y^+(\widehat{\fgl}_1)$.

\item \textbf{Toric CY$_3$: proved via the CoHA.}
For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The correspondence holds at the motivic level because the CoHA multiplication IS the bar differential, and the CoHA Hilbert series IS the bar Euler characteristic.

\item \textbf{$K3 \times E$: observational at genus~$1$, motivic level requires $\Phi_3$.}
The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The shadow data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)/1$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The genus-$1$ shadow $F_1 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$ matches the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.

\item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}
The quintic has $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = -25/3$ (this is one of the cases where $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24$ holds; Theorem~\ref{thm:chi-neq-kappa}). The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches. The BCOV holomorphic anomaly equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:hae-mc-structural}) provides the higher-genus comparison, but the existence of $A_X$ as an $\Eone$-chiral algebra for the quintic depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.

 succeeded in 51ms:
For $K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}). For Enriques~$\times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 2$ (72 tests via \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py}). The general formula $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi^{\CY}(\cC)$ for arbitrary compact CY$_3$ is conditional on the functor itself.

\item \textbf{DT/shadow correspondence at genus~$1$.} \textsc{[Verified for $\C^3$ and $K3 \times E$]}
Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:dt-hocolim-shadow}} predicts $F_1^{\DT}(X) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_X)/24$. For $\C^3$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ gives $F_1 = 1/24$, matching the MacMahon function (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:kappa-c3}}). For $K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ and the genus-$1$ shadow matches.

\item \textbf{Drinfeld center identification for $\C^3$.} \textsc{[Proved]}
$\cZ(\Rep^{\Eone}(Y^+(\widehat{\fgl}_1))) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(Y(\widehat{\fgl}_1)) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(\cW_{1+\infty})$ (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:c3-drinfeld-center}}). This is the mechanism by which the non-symmetric braiding arises from the $\Eone$ structure without native $\Etwo$.

universal algebra generated by the modes of

-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. + \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By the same product-additivity heuristic used for $K3 \times E$, one expects $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
 \end{enumerate}
 The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.
 \end{conjecture}

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

**Inspecting file line endings**

I’m wondering if the issue could be related to file line endings. Maybe breaking it down into smaller sections could help, focusing on exact line 181 and its context. It seems that the `apply_patch` function isn’t matching due to some complication. I think I should inspect the lines using the command `sed -n '176,183p' | cat -vet` to get a clearer view of what's happening.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '176,183p' chapters/examples/quantum_group_reps.tex | cat -vet" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 52ms:
\label{rem:kl-bar-complex}$
From the bar-complex perspective, the KL equivalence states that$
the $\Eone$-representation category of the affine vertex algebra$
$V_k(\frakg)$ recovers the quantum group representation category.$
The Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\frakg))$ encodes the quantum group$
$R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component; the DK bridge (Volume~II,$
MC3 for all simple types) extends this to the full categorical$
equivalence.$

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

**Figuring out patch issues**

I'm wondering if the apply_patch function has trouble with backslashes. But after a moment of thought, I think it might not be that. Perhaps I need to approach this more atomically and focus on making single line replacements instead. It feels like breaking it down could help me see what’s going wrong more clearly! Let’s see if this alternative method works better for me.

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

 For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The correspondence holds at the motivic level because the CoHA multiplication IS the bar differential, and the CoHA Hilbert series IS the bar Euler characteristic.
 
 \item \textbf{$K3 \times E$: observational at genus~$1$, motivic level requires $\Phi_3$.}
-The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The shadow data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)/1$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The genus-$1$ shadow $F_1 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$ matches the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.
+The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The genus-$1$ data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The shadow scalar is therefore $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the automorphic/BKM genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$, matching the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.
 
 \item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}
 The quintic has $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = -25/3$ (this is one of the cases where $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24$ holds; Theorem~\ref{thm:chi-neq-kappa}). The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches. The BCOV holomorphic anomaly equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:hae-mc-structural}) provides the higher-genus comparison, but the existence of $A_X$ as an $\Eone$-chiral algebra for the quintic depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.

+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

chapters/theory/introduction.tex:286: \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:300: \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 2$: steps 1--3 of $\Phi$ (cyclic $\Ainf \to$ Lie conformal $\to$ factorization envelope $\to$ $E_2$ enhancement via $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing).
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:323: \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 3$: the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing and BV-compatibility are not yet established.

+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.
 

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '296,325p' chapters/theory/introduction.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 53ms:
\noindent\textbf{Proved (would survive a referee):}
\begin{itemize}
 \item The generalized root datum axioms CY1--CY7, adequate for $K3 \times E$ and toric CY3 examples.
 \item The $E_2$-chiral algebra formalism and its basic properties (definition, bar complex, braiding).
 \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 2$: steps 1--3 of $\Phi$ (cyclic $\Ainf \to$ Lie conformal $\to$ factorization envelope $\to$ $E_2$ enhancement via $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing).
 \item The CoHA as $E_1$-sector for toric CY3 (via Schiffmann--Vasserot, RSYZ).
 \item The Drinfeld center equivalence $\cZ(\Rep^{E_1}(A)) \simeq \Rep^{E_2}(\Zder(A))$ (Ben-Zvi--Francis--Nadler, Lurie).
 \item All lattice theory and BKM constructions for $K3 \times E$ (Gritsenko--Nikulin).
 \item The denominator identity $\Delta_5 = \Phi$ and product formula (degree 2--6 computationally verified).
 \item The shadow--Siegel gap theorem with four structural obstructions (O1--O4).
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3 \times E) = 3$, verified by six independent paths.
 \item The moonshine multiplier: $A_n = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} \cdot \dim(\rho_n)$ identifies the factor $2$ in $Z_{K3} = 2\,\phi_{0,1}$.
 \item The categorical $\Etwo$-obstruction for CY$_3$ (Proposition~\ref{prop:e1-obstruction-categorical}): Serre duality forces antisymmetry of the Euler form, universally obstructing $\Etwo$-enhancement without torus hypotheses.
 \item Quiver-chart gluing for toric CY$_3$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}): the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra is assembled as a homotopy colimit of McKay-chart CoHAs, with $E_2$ descent degeneration and Costello--Li comparison.
 \item The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$: the finite-dimensional analogue of the DDCA in which $\bC[u,v]$ is replaced by $H^*(S,\bC)$ with the Mukai pairing.
 \item Forty-two CY/BKM compute engines with $5{,}700{+}$ tests across the standard CY landscape.
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textbf{Rigorous proof sketches (completable with effort):}
\begin{itemize}

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

+$d = 3$ via CY-A$_3$.
 \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
   \item \emph{At $d = 2$ (unconditional):} For a CY$_2$ category
   (e.g.\ $D^b(\Coh(K3))$), the functor $\Phi$ produces an
-  $\Etwo$-chiral algebra (Theorem~CY-A at $d = 2$), and the
+  $\Etwo$-chiral algebra (Theorem~CY-A$_2$), and the
   factorization-homology coproduct is the excision map for any
   collar-gluing of the underlying surface.  This recovers the
   lattice vertex algebra coproduct (primitive on generators)
diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..06796f6aa41b742c687a650341e14463eb4d4de9
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]

+ \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By the same product-additivity heuristic used for $K3 \times E$, one expects $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
 \end{enumerate}
 The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.
 \end{conjecture}

The general formula $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi^{\CY}(\cC)$ for arbitrary compact CY$_3$ is conditional on the functor itself.

+Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:dt-hocolim-shadow}} predicts $F_1^{\DT}(X) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_X)/24$. For $\C^3$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ gives $F_1 = 1/24$, matching the MacMahon function (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:kappa-c3}}). For $K3 \times E$: the shadow scalar is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the observed DT genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$; reconciling the latter requires the BKM lift rather than the scalar shadow alone.
 
 \item \textbf{Drinfeld center identification for $\C^3$.} \textsc{[Proved]}
 $\cZ(\Rep^{\Eone}(Y^+(\widehat{\fgl}_1))) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(Y(\widehat{\fgl}_1)) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(\cW_{1+\infty})$ (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:c3-drinfeld-center}}). This is the mechanism by which the non-symmetric braiding arises from the $\Eone$ structure without native $\Etwo$.

 For the resolved conifold: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 2$ (the total space deformation retracts onto $\bP^1$) gives $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = 1/12$, but the DT computation gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ (the conifold has a single BPS state contributing at genus~$1$).

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

  \item Conjecture CY-C: the CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not constructed in general.
  \item The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ does not yet exist as a constructed object; the identification of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ with a chiral algebra modular characteristic is an observation, not a theorem.

 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}
