 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

\label{constr:fukaya-phi}
For a CY manifold $X$ of dimension $d$, the quantum chiral algebra
$A_{\Fuk(X)} = \Phi(\Fuk(X))$ is assembled in three steps
(matching the abstract construction of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ in the
proved $d = 2$ case, and of the CY-A$_3$ programme at $d = 3$):
\begin{enumerate}[label=\arabic*.]
 \item \emph{Cyclic $\Ainf$-extraction}: The Fukaya $\Ainf$-structure
 $\{\mu_k\}_{k \geq 1}$ and Poincar\'{e} pairing give the input
 cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra (Chapter~\ref{ch:cyclic-ainf});
 succeeded in 52ms:
$A_\cC$; the $\Sd$-framing enhances the factorization
structure. For $d = 2$, the $\mathbb{S}^2$-action on
Hochschild homology gives an $\Etwo$-chiral algebra with
braided monoidal representation category, and all three steps
are proved (Theorem~CY-A$_2$). For $d = 3$, holomorphic
Chern--Simons breaks $\Etwo$ to~$\Eone$; the braiding is
recovered via the Drinfeld center of the $\Eone$-monoidal

 succeeded in 50ms:
\label{sec:modular-conv-cy}

Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper cyclic $A_\infty$-category of CY dimension $d$ and let $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ denote the image under the CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi$ of Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ is proved for $d = 2$; $d = 3$ is the Vol~III programme via Conjecture~CY-A$_3$, AP-CY6). The bar coalgebra $B(A_\cC)$ is a factorization coalgebra on $\Ran(C)$ for a fixed smooth projective curve $C$, with bar differential $d_B = d_1 + d_2 + \cdots$ where $d_k$ lowers bar degree by $k - 1$.

\begin{definition}[CY modular convolution algebra]
\label{def:cy-modular-convolution}
The \emph{CY modular convolution algebra} of the pair $(B(A_\cC), A_\cC)$ is the graded vector space
\[
 \mathrm{Conv}_{\mathrm{str}}\bigl(B(A_\cC), A_\cC\bigr)
 \;:=\; \Hom_{\Ran(C)}\!\bigl(B(A_\cC),\, A_\cC \otimes \omega^{\bullet}_{\overline{\cM}_{g,n}}\bigr),
\]
equipped with the convolution bracket induced by the deconcatenation coproduct on $B(A_\cC)$ (dual to $T^c$ on $s^{-1}\overline{A_\cC}$) and the binary product of $A_\cC$, twisted by the modular convolution kernel from $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$. The differential is $\delta = d_B^\vee + d_{A_\cC} + d_{\overline{\cM}}$, where $d_{\overline{\cM}}$ is the log Fulton--MacPherson ambient boundary differential of Mok~\cite{Mok25}.
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[Strict dg Lie structure on $\mathrm{Conv}_{\mathrm{str}}$]
\label{prop:cy-conv-dglie}
\ClaimStatusProvedElsewhere
On the Koszul locus of $A_\cC$, the convolution bracket $[\cdot, \cdot]_{\mathrm{Conv}}$ on $\mathrm{Conv}_{\mathrm{str}}(B(A_\cC), A_\cC)$ satisfies the graded Jacobi identity strictly, and $\delta$ is a derivation of this bracket. The pair $(\mathrm{Conv}_{\mathrm{str}}, \delta, [\cdot, \cdot])$ is a dg Lie algebra; it is a strict model of an $L_\infty$ algebra $\mathrm{Conv}_{\infty}(B(A_\cC), A_\cC)$, and the two share the same Maurer--Cartan moduli space (Vol~I, three-pillar constraint, \S MC3).
\end{proposition}

 \item The Drinfeld center equivalence $\cZ(\Rep^{E_1}(A)) \simeq \Rep^{E_2}(\Zder(A))$ (Ben-Zvi--Francis--Nadler, Lurie).

\end{itemize}

 succeeded in 51ms:
For $K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}). For Enriques~$\times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 2$ (72 tests via \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py}). The general formula $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi^{\CY}(\cC)$ for arbitrary compact CY$_3$ is conditional on the functor itself.

\item \textbf{DT/shadow correspondence at genus~$1$.} \textsc{[Verified for $\C^3$ and $K3 \times E$]}
Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:dt-hocolim-shadow}} predicts $F_1^{\DT}(X) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_X)/24$. For $\C^3$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ gives $F_1 = 1/24$, matching the MacMahon function (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:kappa-c3}}). For $K3 \times E$: the shadow scalar is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the observed DT genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$; reconciling the latter requires the BKM lift rather than the scalar shadow alone.

\item \textbf{Drinfeld center identification for $\C^3$.} \textsc{[Proved]}
 succeeded in 50ms:
For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The correspondence holds at the motivic level because the CoHA multiplication IS the bar differential, and the CoHA Hilbert series IS the bar Euler characteristic.

\item \textbf{$K3 \times E$: observational at genus~$1$, motivic level requires $\Phi_3$.}
The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The genus-$1$ data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The shadow scalar is therefore $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the automorphic/BKM genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$, matching the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.

\item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}
The quintic has $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = -25/3$ (this is one of the cases where $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24$ holds; Theorem~\ref{thm:chi-neq-kappa}). The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches. The BCOV holomorphic anomaly equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:hae-mc-structural}) provides the higher-genus comparison, but the existence of $A_X$ as an $\Eone$-chiral algebra for the quintic depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.
 succeeded in 50ms:
Let $S$ be an Enriques surface, $X$ its K3 universal cover, $E$ an elliptic curve, and assume Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:phi-enriques-z2-orbifold}} and the extension of $\Phi$ to generalized CY$_3$ inputs. Then the three modular characteristics satisfy:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) = 1 = \chi(\cO_S)$, half the K3 value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(X) = 2$.
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 4$, the weight of the Allcock Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$. This is verified computationally by \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py} (72 tests; see Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:enriques-kappa-anomaly}} in the bar-cobar bridge chapter).
 \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By the same product-additivity heuristic used for $K3 \times E$, one expects $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
\end{enumerate}
The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.
 succeeded in 51ms:

Part~\ref{part:cy-categories} establishes the categorical foundations: CY categories, cyclic $\Ainf$-structures, the Hochschild calculus, and the $E_1$/$E_2$/$E_n$ chiral hierarchy with the framing obstruction tower. Part~\ref{part:bridge} constructs the bridge functor $\Phi$ from CY categories to quantum chiral algebras and proves Theorems CY-A (for $d = 2$) and CY-D, with the identification of automorphic correction and shadow obstruction tower as the main structural comparison. Part~\ref{part:examples} treats quantum groups and their braided factorization structure, including the Drinfeld center as the bulk algebra. Part~\ref{part:connections} works through the standard landscape: the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter develops the $K3 \times E$ programme (K3-1 through K3-13, programmes A through J), alongside toric CY3 CoHAs, Fukaya categories, derived categories, matrix factorizations (ADE singularities and W-algebras), and quantum group representations (Kazhdan--Lusztig, affine Yangians, critical CoHAs). Part~\ref{part:frontier} connects to the geometric Langlands programme and to Volumes~I and~II via the bar-cobar bridge and the modular Koszul bridge. Open frontier material is kept separate from these chapters.

\section{Conventions and notation}
\label{sec:conventions-intro}

This volume inherits the conventions of Volumes~I and~II; we record here the points where the CY setting introduces additional notation or where conventions differ across the literature. A comprehensive conventions appendix is Appendix~\ref{app:conventions}.
 succeeded in 50ms:

 succeeded in 51ms:
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Evidence for the $d \geq 3$ conjecture]
\label{rem:cy-kappa-evidence}
At $d = 2$, the identification is proved (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:cy-kappa-d2}}). At $d = 3$, the conjecture is verified for $\C^3$ where both sides are independently computable ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} = 1$; see Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:kappa-c3}}). The obstruction to extending the $d = 2$ proof is that the $d = 3$ quantization step may introduce corrections: Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [3]$ no longer kills the one-loop anomaly by parity. The $d = 3$ functor itself is conjectural beyond $\C^3$.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Enriques surface: orbifold and kappa halving]
\label{rem:enriques-orbifold-kappa}
 succeeded in 51ms:
 \item\label{concl:bar} \emph{Bar identification}: $B^{\Eone}(\Phi_3(\cC)) \simeq \mathrm{CC}_\bullet^{\Eone}(\cC)$ as $\Eone$-factorization coalgebras, extending Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:cy-to-chiral}(ii)} from $d = 2$.
 \item\label{concl:e1} \emph{Native $\Eone$ structure}: $A_\cC$ is natively $\Eone$, not $\Etwo$. The $\Etwo$-braided structure on $\Rep(A_\cC)$ arises through the Drinfeld center $\cZ(\Rep^{\Eone}(A_\cC))$, not from the algebra itself \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:e1-universality-cy3}} for toric input\textup{)}.
 \item\label{concl:kappa} \emph{Modular characteristic}: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC) = \chi^{\CY}(\cC) := \sum_i (-1)^i \dim \HH_i(\cC)$, the categorical Euler characteristic \textup{(}Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:cy-kappa-identification}}\textup{)}.
 \item\label{concl:shadow} \emph{Shadow tower}: $A_\cC$ carries the full shadow obstruction tower of Vol~I, with $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$-invariance guaranteed by bar-hocolim commutation \textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:bar-hocolim}}\textup{)}.
\end{enumerate}
 succeeded in 51ms:
\label{prop:categorical-euler}
\ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.

The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.

Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
 \item Weight of the Igusa cusp form: $\mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5) = 5$.
 \item DMVV exponent: the Borcherds lift weight formula gives $c(0)/2 = 10/2 = 5$.
 \item Bar Euler product: the genus-$1$ coefficient of $\log Z_{\DT}$ gives $F_1 = 5/24$.
 \item Relative DT: fiber-by-fiber computation via K3 fibers sewed along $E$.
 \item Anomaly cancellation: the worldsheet anomaly for the $K3 \times E$ sigma model.

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

The obstruction to extending the $d = 2$ proof is that the $d = 3$ quantization step may introduce corrections: Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [3]$ no longer kills the one-loop anomaly by parity. The $d = 3$ functor itself is conjectural beyond $\C^3$.
 \end{remark}

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.
 


thinking
**Considering heading clarity**

If the user specifically asked about d=2 only, it might be safer to change the heading to emphasize that — perhaps to `\item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture CY-A$_3$)`. That way, it's more explicit. I also noticed that the guideline already mentions Theorems CY-A (for d=2) and CY-D, which is fine. It seems like a good direction to clarify what I'm presenting!

@@ -285,3 +285,3 @@
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.

The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. +The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The genus-$1$ data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The shadow scalar is therefore $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the automorphic/BKM genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$, matching the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.
 
 \item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}
 The quintic has $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = -25/3$ (this is one of the cases where $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24$ holds; Theorem~\ref{thm:chi-neq-kappa}). The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches. The BCOV holomorphic anomaly equation (Theorem~\ref{thm:hae-mc-structural}) provides the higher-genus comparison, but the existence of $A_X$ as an $\Eone$-chiral algebra for the quintic depends on the CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$.

 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

+ \item Conjecture CY-A$_3$: the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing and BV-compatibility are not yet established.
  \item Conjecture CY-C: the CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not constructed in general.
  \item The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ does not yet exist as a constructed object; the identification of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ with a chiral algebra modular characteristic is an observation, not a theorem.
  \item The shadow--BPS/DT correspondence (Conjecture~\ref{conj:shadow-bps-dt}): three-level identification of the shadow partition function with DT invariants, from genus-$1$ through virtual to motivic.
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
  \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) = 1 = \chi(\cO_S)$, half the K3 value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(X) = 2$.
  \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 4$, the weight of the Allcock Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$. This is verified computationally by \texttt{enriques\_shadow.py} (72 tests; see Remark~\textup{\ref{rem:enriques-kappa-anomaly}} in the bar-cobar bridge chapter).
- \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By additivity (Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:categorical-euler}}), $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
+ \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By the same product-additivity heuristic used for $K3 \times E$, one expects $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.
 \end{enumerate}
 The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.

The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The genus-$1$ DT free energy $F_1 = -25/72$ matches.  \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1723:The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The genus-$1$ data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The shadow scalar is therefore $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the automorphic/BKM genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$, matching the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.

chapters/theory/introduction.tex:244:The combinatorics of a Calabi--Yau threefold $X$ (lattice, intersection form, BPS spectrum) constitute the \emph{generalized root datum} of a \emph{quantum vertex chiral group} $G(X)$. For toric CY3 (via the CoHA and Drinfeld double) and for CY$_2$ categories (via Theorem~CY-A$_2$), this object is constructed. For general CY$_3$, including the prototype $K3 \times E$, $G(X)$ is the target of a programme (Conjecture~CY-A$_3$), not a constructed object.
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:286: \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:524:\emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.

chapters/examples/derived_categories_cy.tex:126:The $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$-equality is verified unconditionally at $d = 1$: for mirror elliptic curves $E_\tau, E_{-1/\tau}$, both sides produce the Heisenberg vertex algebra and $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}} = 1 = \chi(\cO_E)$. At $d = 2$, the equality reduces via Theorem~CY-A$_2$ to the comparison of Euler characteristics $\chi(\cO_X) = \chi(\cO_{X^\vee})$, which holds because mirror K3 surfaces share the same underlying smooth manifold. At $d = 3$, the equality is conjectural: it awaits both the construction of $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ and an independent comparison of $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$ across the HMS equivalence. The chiral restatement transports $R$-matrices between the A-side and the B-side. On the A-side, the $R$-matrix comes from Floer-theoretic intersection pairings; on the B-side, from Ext-pairings and the Grothendieck residue. The mirror map intertwines them.

+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

+ \item Conjecture CY-A$_3$: the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing and BV-compatibility are not yet established.
  \item Conjecture CY-C: the CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not constructed in general.
  \item The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ does not yet exist as a constructed object; the identification of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ with a chiral algebra modular characteristic is an observation, not a theorem.
  \item The shadow--BPS/DT correspondence (Conjecture~\ref{conj:shadow-bps-dt}): three-level identification of the shadow partition function with DT invariants, from genus-$1$ through virtual to motivic.
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.
 


thinking
**Searching for problematic patterns**

I’m planning to search the entire repository for problematic patterns, not just in modified files. Specifically, I want to focus on instances of "Theorem CY-A at d=3," "MC3 for all simple types," and any misuse of kappa_BKM. It’s essential to check all chapters to ensure I capture everything. This way, I'll get a comprehensive understanding of any issues present throughout the repo. Let's dive into that search!
codex
The remaining cross-volume references in `chapters/` are numerous but mostly factual citations, not stale ones. I’m doing one last repo-wide sweep for the concrete stale patterns the audit highlighted, so I can confirm there aren’t unresolved copies outside the files I edited.

chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1723:The CY-to-chiral functor at $d = 3$ is not yet constructed for $K3 \times E$ (Conjecture~\ref{conj:cy-to-chiral-d3}). The genus-$1$ data is read from two sources: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$; Proposition~\ref{prop:categorical-euler}), and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$ from the Borcherds lift weight formula. The shadow scalar is therefore $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}/24 = 3/24$, while the automorphic/BKM genus-$1$ coefficient is $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}/24 = 5/24$, matching the DT genus-$1$ free energy. The full DT partition function $Z_{\DT}(K3 \times E) = C/\Delta_5^2$ involves the Igusa cusp form, whose Borcherds product formula $\Delta_5 = p \prod (1-p^n q^l r^m)^{f(4nm - l^2)}$ is a three-variable generalisation of the bar Euler product (Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}). The passage from the scalar shadow to the full $\Delta_5$ requires the BKM root system, which encodes all BPS states across all charge lattice directions.
chapters/theory/cy_to_chiral.tex:1861:Conjecture~\textup{\ref{conj:dt-hocolim-shadow}} predicts $F_1^{\DT}(X) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_X)/24$. chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:67:a two-dimensional $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on one generator. The Jacobi ring is $\Jac(x^2) = \C[x]/(2x) \cong \C$, so $\HH_\bullet(\MF(x^2)) \cong \C$ is one-dimensional; the two-dimensional endomorphism algebra counts the $\Z/2$-graded indecomposable and its parity shift. The dimension count $2$ is the rank count of the free-fermion representation: a single holomorphic fermion contributes a two-dimensional Clifford sector, and this is the smallest nonzero input to the LG-to-chiral passage at the level of Hochschild invariants. The $n = 1$ case sits outside the CY$_2$ domain of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Remark~\ref{rem:mf-dim-caveat}) and is used only as the building block for the stabilized four-variable model in Section~\ref{sec:ade-w-algebras}.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:72:Let $W \colon \C^4 \to \C$ have an isolated critical point at the origin. By Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} the category $\MF(W)$ is CY$_2$, hence Theorem~CY-A$_2$ applies and produces a chiral algebra
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:81:Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}) applies to any smooth proper CY$_2$ category. The value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi(\HH_\bullet)$ is the CY Euler characteristic clause of that theorem; Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-hh} identifies $\HH_\bullet$ with $\Jac(W)$ placed in a single parity, whose Euler characteristic is the $\C$-dimension of the Jacobi ring, namely $\mu(W)$.

chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:108:Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (should it exist) depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The modular characteristic equality follows from the CY Euler characteristic clause of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}), which depends only on $\HH_\bullet$.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:113:Theorem~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Here we only transport the statement already proved at $d = 2$: $\Phi$ sends cyclic $A_\infty$-objects to chiral algebras compatibly with the bar construction (Theorem~CY-A$_2$(ii)); the $d = 3$ analogue remains part of CY-A$_3$. The convolution bracket on the CY side is therefore pulled back from the chiral convolution bracket of Vol~I on the proved surface.
 \end{proof}
