 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

One additional `CY-A` scope leak turned up outside the earlier patch set: a theorem statement in `quantum_groups_foundations.tex` still referred to “Theorem~CY-A$_d$” as if the functor were proved uniformly in `d`. I’m patching that single spot and then rerunning the narrow scope check.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '228,242p' chapters/theory/quantum_groups_foundations.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groupsexec
/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n -F "CY-A$_d" chapters' in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 51ms:

The Vol~III main conjecture CY-C upgrades Theorem~\ref{thm:qgf-kazhdan-lusztig} from affine Kac--Moody data to CY-category data. We recall the precise statement and scope.

\begin{conjecture}[CY-C: quantum group realization]
\label{conj:qgf-cy-c}
\ClaimStatusConjectured
Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper CY-$d$ category ($d = 2$ or $d = 3$) satisfying the hypotheses of Theorem~CY-A$_d$ (Chapter~\ref{ch:cy-to-chiral}), and let $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ be its image under the CY-to-chiral functor. There exists a parameter $q \in \C^*$ (determined by $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC)$) and a Hopf algebra in a braided category, $C(\cC, q)$, such that there is an equivalence of modular tensor categories
\begin{equation}
 \Rep^{\mathrm{fd}}(C(\cC, q)) \ \simeq \ \mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)),
 \label{eq:qgf-cy-c}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)) := \Rep^{\Etwo}(A_\cC)^{\mathrm{ss}}$ is the (semisimplified) conformal-block category of $A_\cC$. When $\cC = D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(\mathrm{pt}/G))$ for $G$ a simple algebraic group, one recovers $C(\cC, q) = U_q(\mathrm{Lie}\, G)$ and Theorem~\ref{thm:qgf-kazhdan-lusztig}.
\end{conjecture}

\begin{remark}[Scope by CY dimension]

chapters/examples/toric_cy3_coha.tex:6:For $d = 2$, the question would be settled by Theorem~CY-A$_2$ directly. For $d = 3$, it is the programme: $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ is conditional on the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing, so any claim about the resulting chiral algebra must be tagged accordingly. What is unconditional is the CoHA side. The toric diagram of $X_\Sigma$ determines a quiver with potential $(Q_X, W_X)$; the critical CoHA is $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X) = \bigoplus_\mathbf{d} H^{\mathrm{BM}}_*(\mathrm{Crit}(W_\mathbf{d}), \phi_{W_\mathbf{d}})$; the theorems of Schiffmann--Vasserot ($\C^3$) and Rapcak--Soibelman--Yang--Zhao (general toric CY3 without compact $4$-cycles) identify $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X)$ with the positive half $Y^+(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X})$ of the affine super Yangian attached to the toric quiver.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:4855: \item \textbf{(Conjecture.)} There exists a chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X)))$ whose bar complex $B(A)$ encodes the product formula for $\Delta_5$. \emph{Note}: the functor $\Phi$ is constructed for $d = 2$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$); the $d = 3$ case (which applies here, since $K3 \times E$ is CY$_3$) is the content of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:4955:The abstract functor $\Phi \colon D^b(\Coh(X)) \to \text{ChirAlg}$ of Conjecture CY-A$_3$ can be bypassed for $K3 \times E$ by exploiting the elliptic fibration $\pi \colon S \to \bP^1$ directly.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:5132: \item \textbf{CY$_3$ quantum vertex chiral group (Conjecture CY-A$_3$).} Apply the abstract CY-to-chiral functor $\Phi \colon D^b(\Coh(K3 \times E)) \to \text{ChirAlg}$ of Conjecture CY-A$_3$. This gives $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\Coh(K3 \times E)))$ directly, with $G(K3 \times E) = G(A_{K3 \times E})$. \emph{Status}: conjectural (CY-A$_3$ is not yet proved; the $d=2$ case CY-A$_2$ is proved).
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:5139:Routes 1--3 give progressively more detailed structural information: Route 1 captures the combinatorial skeleton ($\fn_+$ and the root system), Route 2 gives the generic (abelian) fiber, and Route 3 gives the enhanced (nonabelian) fiber. Route 4 would give the full chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ but requires CY-A$_3$. Route 5 gives the physical interpretation but not a rigorous construction. The relative chiral algebra of Section~\ref{sec:k3e-relative-chiral} provides a concrete intermediary between Routes 2--3 and Route 4.

chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:67:a two-dimensional $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on one generator. The Jacobi ring is $\Jac(x^2) = \C[x]/(2x) \cong \C$, so $\HH_\bullet(\MF(x^2)) \cong \C$ is one-dimensional; the two-dimensional endomorphism algebra counts the $\Z/2$-graded indecomposable and its parity shift. The dimension count $2$ is the rank count of the free-fermion representation: a single holomorphic fermion contributes a two-dimensional Clifford sector, and this is the smallest nonzero input to the LG-to-chiral passage at the level of Hochschild invariants. The $n = 1$ case sits outside the CY$_2$ domain of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Remark~\ref{rem:mf-dim-caveat}) and is used only as the building block for the stabilized four-variable model in Section~\ref{sec:ade-w-algebras}.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:72:Let $W \colon \C^4 \to \C$ have an isolated critical point at the origin. By Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} the category $\MF(W)$ is CY$_2$, hence Theorem~CY-A$_2$ applies and produces a chiral algebra
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:81:Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}) applies to any smooth proper CY$_2$ category. The value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi(\HH_\bullet)$ is the CY Euler characteristic clause of that theorem; Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-hh} identifies $\HH_\bullet$ with $\Jac(W)$ placed in a single parity, whose Euler characteristic is the $\C$-dimension of the Jacobi ring, namely $\mu(W)$.

 The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant.  For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification.  $\cZ(\Rep^{\Eone}(Y^+(\widehat{\fgl}_1))) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(Y(\widehat{\fgl}_1)) \simeq \Rep^{\Etwo}(\cW_{1+\infty})$ (Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:c3-drinfeld-center}}). This is the mechanism by which the non-symmetric braiding arises from the $\Eone$ structure without native $\Etwo$.

 For the resolved conifold: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 2$ (the total space deformation retracts onto $\bP^1$) gives $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}/24 = 1/12$, but the DT computation gives $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 1$ (the conifold has a single BPS state contributing at genus~$1$).

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]
diff --git a/chapters/theory/cyclic_ainf.tex b/chapters/theory/cyclic_ainf.tex
index dba0395993e193ea3d09f3edfb63ef91b93b4199..32257db0d88243dcd0e0183d10f9ea85c92e3da6
--- a/chapters/theory/cyclic_ainf.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/cyclic_ainf.tex
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.
 
 \section{$\Ainf$-algebras}
 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

@@ -283,7 +283,7 @@
 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

chapters/examples/toric_cy3_coha.tex:6:For $d = 2$, the question would be settled by Theorem~CY-A$_2$ directly. For $d = 3$, it is the programme: $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ is conditional on the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing, so any claim about the resulting chiral algebra must be tagged accordingly. What is unconditional is the CoHA side. The toric diagram of $X_\Sigma$ determines a quiver with potential $(Q_X, W_X)$; the critical CoHA is $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X) = \bigoplus_\mathbf{d} H^{\mathrm{BM}}_*(\mathrm{Crit}(W_\mathbf{d}), \phi_{W_\mathbf{d}})$; the theorems of Schiffmann--Vasserot ($\C^3$) and Rapcak--Soibelman--Yang--Zhao (general toric CY3 without compact $4$-cycles) identify $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X)$ with the positive half $Y^+(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X})$ of the affine super Yangian attached to the toric quiver.
chapters/examples/toroidal_elliptic.tex:4855: \item \textbf{(Conjecture.)} There exists a chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E} = \Phi(D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X)))$ whose bar complex $B(A)$ encodes the product formula for $\Delta_5$. \emph{Note}: the functor $\Phi$ is constructed for $d = 2$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$); the $d = 3$ case (which applies here, since $K3 \times E$ is CY$_3$) is the content of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$.

chapters/examples/fukaya_categories.tex:530:For $d = 2$, the operative input is Theorem~CY-A$_2$: it proves
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:67:a two-dimensional $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on one generator. The Jacobi ring is $\Jac(x^2) = \C[x]/(2x) \cong \C$, so $\HH_\bullet(\MF(x^2)) \cong \C$ is one-dimensional; the two-dimensional endomorphism algebra counts the $\Z/2$-graded indecomposable and its parity shift. The dimension count $2$ is the rank count of the free-fermion representation: a single holomorphic fermion contributes a two-dimensional Clifford sector, and this is the smallest nonzero input to the LG-to-chiral passage at the level of Hochschild invariants. The $n = 1$ case sits outside the CY$_2$ domain of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Remark~\ref{rem:mf-dim-caveat}) and is used only as the building block for the stabilized four-variable model in Section~\ref{sec:ade-w-algebras}.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:72:Let $W \colon \C^4 \to \C$ have an isolated critical point at the origin. By Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} the category $\MF(W)$ is CY$_2$, hence Theorem~CY-A$_2$ applies and produces a chiral algebra
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:81:Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}) applies to any smooth proper CY$_2$ category. The value $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = \chi(\HH_\bullet)$ is the CY Euler characteristic clause of that theorem; Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-hh} identifies $\HH_\bullet$ with $\Jac(W)$ placed in a single parity, whose Euler characteristic is the $\C$-dimension of the Jacobi ring, namely $\mu(W)$.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:99:Let $W$ be a homogeneous polynomial of degree $n + 1$ in $n + 1$ variables with smooth projective zero locus $Q \subset \mathbf{P}^n$. Assume Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (the $d = 3$ CY-to-chiral functor). Then
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:108:Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (should it exist) depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. The modular characteristic equality follows from the CY Euler characteristic clause of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}), which depends only on $\HH_\bullet$.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:113:Theorem~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:130:To obtain a CY$_2$ input for Theorem~CY-A$_2$ one stabilizes by adding two quadratic variables:
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:138:where $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ is the $\Z/2$-graded Clifford algebra on two generators; the dimension count in Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} becomes $n - 2 = 2$, so $\MF(\widetilde{W}_{\mathrm{ADE}})$ is CY$_2$ and $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ applies.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:154:The ADE prediction sits in the image of Theorem~CY-A$_2$, hence the $d = 2$ case where $\Phi$ is proved. What prevents stating it as a theorem is not the functor but the identification of the output with a named $\cW$-algebra: we have no intrinsic construction of $\cW_k(\mathfrak{g})$ from $\HH_\bullet(\MF(\widetilde{W}_{\mathrm{ADE}}))$, only a matching of the modular characteristic and a physical argument for the full structure. Conjecture~\ref{conj:ade-w-algebra} is therefore stated as a prediction, not a proved theorem, in line with AP-CY14.
chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:171:the $\Z/2$-graded category of finite-dimensional vector spaces (the Morita equivalence $\mathrm{Cl}_4 \simeq \C$ removes the Clifford factor because $\mathrm{Cl}_4 \cong M_2(\C)$ as a $\Z/2$-graded algebra, and $M_2(\C)$ is Morita trivial). Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} places $\MF(\widetilde{W}_{A_1})$ in dimension $2$, matching the CY$_2$ hypothesis of Theorem~CY-A$_2$.

chapters/examples/derived_categories_cy.tex:54:For a K3 surface $X$, the Hodge diamond and HKR give $\HH^0 \simeq \C$, $\HH^1 \simeq H^1(X, T_X) \oplus H^0(X, \Omega^1_X) = H^1(X, T_X)$ (of dimension $20$), and $\HH^2 \simeq H^0(X, \Lambda^2 T_X) \oplus H^2(X, \cO_X) \simeq \C \oplus \C$. The CY$_2$ structure $S_X = [2]$ pairs $\HH^2$ with $\HH^0$ by the trace. Under $\Phi$, Theorem~CY-A$_2$ produces the K3 chiral algebra $\cA_{K3}$ of \S\ref{sec:k3-chiral-algebra}: the small $\cN = 4$ superconformal algebra at $c = 6$. chapters/examples/toric_cy3_coha.tex:6:For $d = 2$, the question would be settled by Theorem~CY-A$_2$ directly. For $d = 3$, it is the programme: $\Phi$ at $d = 3$ is conditional on the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing, so any claim about the resulting chiral algebra must be tagged accordingly. What is unconditional is the CoHA side. The toric diagram of $X_\Sigma$ determines a quiver with potential $(Q_X, W_X)$; the critical CoHA is $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X) = \bigoplus_\mathbf{d} H^{\mathrm{BM}}_*(\mathrm{Crit}(W_\mathbf{d}), \phi_{W_\mathbf{d}})$; the theorems of Schiffmann--Vasserot ($\C^3$) and Rapcak--Soibelman--Yang--Zhao (general toric CY3 without compact $4$-cycles) identify $\mathcal{H}(Q_X, W_X)$ with the positive half $Y^+(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X})$ of the affine super Yangian attached to the toric quiver.

chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex:33:The CY dimension of $\MF(W)$ is $n - 2$, not $n - 1$. The shift by two arises from the $\Z/2$-grading: the ambient $n$-dimensional affine space is traded for the $(n-1)$-dimensional critical locus, and a further shift comes from the Serre functor of the periodic category. Consequently ADE singularities in $n = 2$ variables give CY$_0$ (semisimple) categories, and one needs $n = 4$ variables to obtain a CY$_2$ category accessible to the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}).

The obstruction to extending the $d = 2$ proof is that the $d = 3$ quantization step may introduce corrections: Serre duality $\mathbb{S}_\cC \simeq [3]$ no longer kills the one-loop anomaly by parity. The $d = 3$ functor itself is conjectural beyond $\C^3$.
 \end{remark}

+ \item $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \chi^{\CY}(D^b(\Coh(S \times E)))$, the categorical Euler characteristic. By the same product-additivity heuristic used for $K3 \times E$, one expects $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}(S \times E) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(S) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 1 + 1 = 2$.

 The ratio $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(X \times E) / \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(S \times E) = 5/4$ (not $2$) reflects the fact that $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ is the automorphic weight, which is sensitive to the full BPS spectrum across the fiber and not simply the $|G|$-fold quotient of the scalar invariant. The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.

 For any smooth toric CY$_3$ $X_\Sigma$ with McKay quiver atlas $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)_{\alpha \in I}$, the toric chart gluing theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:toric-chart-gluing}) assembles the global $\Eone$-chiral algebra $A_{X_\Sigma}$. The $\CoHA$ of $(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ is the positive half of the quantum vertex chiral group $G(Q_\alpha, W_\alpha)$ (Kontsevich--Soibelman, Schiffmann--Vasserot, Davison). The bar complex of $A_{X_\Sigma}$ recovers the DT moduli via the CoHA-bar identification. The correspondence holds at the motivic level because the CoHA multiplication IS the bar differential, and the CoHA Hilbert series IS the bar Euler characteristic.

 \item \textbf{Quintic: conditional on $\Phi_3$.}

-\begin{proposition}[The categorical Euler characteristic resolves the discrepancy]
+\begin{proposition}[$K3 \times E$: $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$ and $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ are distinct]
 \label{prop:categorical-euler}
 \ClaimStatusProvedHere{}
-The invariant controlling the BKM modular characteristic is the \emph{categorical} Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}$, not the topological one. For $K3 \times E$: $\chi_{\mathrm{top}} = 0$ but $\chi^{\CY} = 5 = \kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$; the chiral modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ arises from additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$).
+For $K3 \times E$, the topological Euler characteristic does not control the genus-$1$ data. The single-copy chiral modular characteristic is $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}} = 3$ by additivity ($\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(K3) + \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(E) = 2 + 1$), while the Borcherds automorphic weight is the distinct invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5 = \mathrm{wt}(\Delta_5)$.
 
-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$ through the Borcherds product data, while the single-copy shadow scalar still records only $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$.
 
 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:ainf-algebras}

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

 \begin{remark}[AP-CY6, AP-CY11: conditionality on the $d = 3$ programme]
 \label{rem:mf-d3-conditional}
-Theorem~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
+Conjecture~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
 \end{remark}
 
 The LG/CY correspondence is the derived-category image of the physics claim that the Landau--Ginzburg theory with superpotential $W$ and the non-linear sigma model on $Q$ are the two phases of a single gauged linear sigma model (Witten \cite{Witten1993}). The Vol~III prediction in Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} is the mathematical assertion that the two phases produce the same chiral algebra, or: that the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ descends to the gauged linear sigma model and does not distinguish its phases.

+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic.  Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:

-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..d7101316126bb241f34f9f93aa1e32b3ba071fbf
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -283,7 +283,7 @@
 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.
@@ -297,7 +297,7 @@
 \begin{itemize}
  \item The generalized root datum axioms CY1--CY7, adequate for $K3 \times E$ and toric CY3 examples.
  \item The $E_2$-chiral algebra formalism and its basic properties (definition, bar complex, braiding).
- \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 2$: steps 1--3 of $\Phi$ (cyclic $\Ainf \to$ Lie conformal $\to$ factorization envelope $\to$ $E_2$ enhancement via $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing).
+ \item Theorem CY-A$_2$: steps 1--3 of $\Phi$ (cyclic $\Ainf \to$ Lie conformal $\to$ factorization envelope $\to$ $E_2$ enhancement via $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing).
  \item The CoHA as $E_1$-sector for toric CY3 (via Schiffmann--Vasserot, RSYZ).
  \item The Drinfeld center equivalence $\cZ(\Rep^{E_1}(A)) \simeq \Rep^{E_2}(\Zder(A))$ (Ben-Zvi--Francis--Nadler, Lurie).
  \item All lattice theory and BKM constructions for $K3 \times E$ (Gritsenko--Nikulin).

 \noindent\textbf{Conjectural:}
 \begin{itemize}
- \item Theorem CY-A for $d = 3$: the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing and BV-compatibility are not yet established.
+ \item Conjecture CY-A$_3$: the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing and BV-compatibility are not yet established.
  \item Conjecture CY-C: the CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not constructed in general.
  \item The chiral algebra $A_{K3 \times E}$ does not yet exist as a constructed object; the identification of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$ with a chiral algebra modular characteristic is an observation, not a theorem.
  \item The shadow--BPS/DT correspondence (Conjecture~\ref{conj:shadow-bps-dt}): three-level identification of the shadow partition function with DT invariants, from genus-$1$ through virtual to motivic.
@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

--- a/chapters/examples/fukaya_categories.tex

@@ -414,7 +414,8 @@
 \label{constr:fukaya-phi}
 For a CY manifold $X$ of dimension $d$, the quantum chiral algebra
 $A_{\Fuk(X)} = \Phi(\Fuk(X))$ is assembled in three steps
-(matching the abstract construction of Theorem~CY-A):
+(matching the abstract construction of Theorem~CY-A$_2$ in the
+proved $d = 2$ case, and of the CY-A$_3$ programme at $d = 3$):

 \begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
-Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (should it exist) depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. +Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$, if it exists, depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. The modular characteristic equality follows from the conjectural CY Euler characteristic clause of CY-A$_3$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}), which depends only on $\HH_\bullet$.
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[AP-CY6, AP-CY11: conditionality on the $d = 3$ programme]
 \label{rem:mf-d3-conditional}
-Theorem~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
+Conjecture~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
 \end{remark}
 
 The LG/CY correspondence is the derived-category image of the physics claim that the Landau--Ginzburg theory with superpotential $W$ and the non-linear sigma model on $Q$ are the two phases of a single gauged linear sigma model (Witten \cite{Witten1993}). The Vol~III prediction in Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} is the mathematical assertion that the two phases produce the same chiral algebra, or: that the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ descends to the gauged linear sigma model and does not distinguish its phases.

-The categorical Euler characteristic accounts for the full BPS spectrum (all charges), not just the massless modes that contribute to $\chi_{\mathrm{top}}$. For K3-fibered CY$_3$, the infinite tower of bound states across fibers contributes to $\chi^{\CY}$ via the Borcherds denominator weight.
+The BKM weight accounts for the full automorphic/BPS package encoded by the Borcherds denominator; it should not be identified with the chiral modular characteristic. index bbf551b8550c07bcfc6afbcd1ccbc0843ded8548..d7101316126bb241f34f9f93aa1e32b3ba071fbf
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.

@@ -523,7 +523,7 @@
 
 \emph{Matrix factorizations}. The Landau--Ginzburg input. A polynomial $W \colon \mathbb{C}^n \to \mathbb{C}$ gives a CY category $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ of dimension $n - 2$. For ADE singularities $W = x^N + y^2 + z^2 + w^2$ in four variables, $\mathrm{MF}(W)$ is CY of dimension~$2$ and $\Phi$ (Theorem~CY-A$_2$) produces chiral algebras related to $\mathcal{W}_N$-algebras. The LG/CY correspondence $\mathrm{MF}(W) \simeq D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(X_W))$ provides a further consistency check against the derived-category side. For non-ADE singularities (unimodal, bimodal), the resulting chiral algebras are expected to be new objects not realized by the standard Lie-theoretic landscape of Volume~I.
 
-\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
+\emph{Quantum group representations} (Chapter~\ref{ch:quantum-group-reps}). The representation-theoretic target. The braided monoidal category $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g})$ is what the CY programme aims to realize: Conjecture~CY-C predicts that $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{Rep}^{E_2}(A_\cC)$ for a CY category $\cC(\mathfrak{g}, q)$. The Kazhdan--Lusztig equivalence $\mathrm{Rep}_q(\mathfrak{g}) \simeq \mathrm{KL}_k(\mathfrak{g})$ at roots of unity is the prototype: the Volume~I bar complex $B(V_k(\mathfrak{g}))$ encodes the quantum group $R$-matrix in its degree-$(1,1)$ component, and the DK bridge (Volume~II, MC3 on the evaluation-generated core for all simple types) recovers the quantum group categorical structure used here. The affine Yangian realization (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) provides the $E_1$-sector; the Drinfeld center (Chapter~\ref{ch:drinfeld-center}) provides the $E_2$-enhancement.
 
 In addition to the four standard families, the toroidal and elliptic algebras chapter (Chapter~\ref{chap:toroidal-elliptic}) develops the $K3 \times E$ programme in full: the generalized root datum, the BKM superalgebra $\mathfrak{g}_{\Delta_5}$ with its Igusa cusp form denominator identity, the shadow--Siegel gap theorem, thirteen structural results (K3-1 through K3-13), all ten research programmes (A through J), and the DDCA--toroidal bridge identifying the deformed double current algebra as the rational degeneration of the quantum toroidal algebra (Conjecture~\ref{conj:ddca-toroidal-bridge}). The K3 double current algebra $\fg_{K3}$, discussed in the DDCA--toroidal bridge of Chapter~\ref{ch:k3-times-e}, provides the K3 analogue of the DDCA, replacing $\bC[u,v]$ with $H^*(S,\bC)$. The toric CY$_3$ CoHA chapter (Chapter~\ref{ch:toric-coha}) develops the affine Yangian and $\mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$ side: the Jordan quiver gives $Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{gl}}_1) \simeq \mathcal{W}_{1+\infty}$, the toric fan is the Dynkin data, and the shuffle algebra presentation connects to the KZ equations of Volume~I.

I’ve applied the last scope patch. Now I’m rereading the exact edited passages and rerunning the grep with shell-safe quoting so the final status is based on the actual file contents, not on expanded `$` tokens.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '232,240p' chapters/theory/quantum_groups_foundations.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groupsexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '94,116p' chapters/examples/matrix_factorizations.tex" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groupsexec
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n -F 'Theorem~CY-A"'$_3$'"' chapters" in /Users/raeez/calabi-yau-quantum-groups succeeded in 52ms:
\label{conj:qgf-cy-c}
\ClaimStatusConjectured
Let $\cC$ be a smooth proper CY-$d$ category ($d = 2$ or $d = 3$), with $A_\cC = \Phi(\cC)$ defined by Theorem~CY-A$_2$ when $d = 2$ and conjecturally by CY-A$_3$ when $d = 3$. There exists a parameter $q \in \C^*$ (determined by $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_\cC)$) and a Hopf algebra in a braided category, $C(\cC, q)$, such that there is an equivalence of modular tensor categories
\begin{equation}
 \Rep^{\mathrm{fd}}(C(\cC, q)) \ \simeq \ \mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)),
 \label{eq:qgf-cy-c}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{MTC}(\Phi(\cC)) := \Rep^{\Etwo}(A_\cC)^{\mathrm{ss}}$ is the (semisimplified) conformal-block category of $A_\cC$. When $\cC = D^b(\mathrm{Coh}(\mathrm{pt}/G))$ for $G$ a simple algebraic group, one recovers $C(\cC, q) = U_q(\mathrm{Lie}\, G)$ and Theorem~\ref{thm:qgf-kazhdan-lusztig}.
\end{conjecture}

\]
while at the singular locus of $W$ the ungraded category $\MF(W)$ recovers the singularity category of the affine cone. The dimension count matches: $W \colon \C^5 \to \C$ has $n = 5$, so by Theorem~\ref{thm:mf-cy-dimension} the ungraded category $\MF(W)$ is CY$_3$, agreeing with the CY$_3$ dimension of $D^b(\Coh(Q))$. The grading shift interpolates between the two and identifies them as $\C^\times$-equivariant CY categories of dimension $3$.

\begin{proposition}[LG/CY matching of Vol~III inputs; conditional on CY-A$_3$]
\label{prop:lg-cy-matching}
Let $W$ be a homogeneous polynomial of degree $n + 1$ in $n + 1$ variables with smooth projective zero locus $Q \subset \mathbf{P}^n$. Assume Conjecture~CY-A$_3$ (the conjectural $d = 3$ CY-to-chiral functor). Then
\[
 \Phi\bigl(\MF^{\gr}(W)_{0}\bigr) \;\simeq\; \Phi\bigl(D^b(\Coh(Q))\bigr)
\]
as chiral algebras. In particular, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\MF^{\gr}(W)_0)) = \kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(D^b(\Coh(Q))))$.
\ClaimStatusConditional
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$, if it exists, depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. The modular characteristic equality follows from the conjectural CY Euler characteristic clause of CY-A$_3$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}), which depends only on $\HH_\bullet$.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[AP-CY6, AP-CY11: conditionality on the $d = 3$ programme]
\label{rem:mf-d3-conditional}
Conjecture~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.

 \begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
-Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Theorem~CY-A$_3$ (should it exist) depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. +Orlov's theorem is an equivalence of CY$_3$ dg-categories; the functor $\Phi$ of Conjecture~CY-A$_3$, if it exists, depends only on the CY dg-equivalence class of its input. The two sides agree as inputs, so their images under $\Phi$ agree. The modular characteristic equality follows from the conjectural CY Euler characteristic clause of CY-A$_3$ (Section~\ref{sec:cy-chiral-functor}), which depends only on $\HH_\bullet$.
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[AP-CY6, AP-CY11: conditionality on the $d = 3$ programme]
 \label{rem:mf-d3-conditional}
-Theorem~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
+Conjecture~CY-A$_3$ is a programme, not a theorem: the chain-level $\bS^3$-framing and global existence of $A_X$ for a compact CY$_3$ are open. Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} therefore carries \texttt{ClaimStatusConditional} with CY-A$_3$ as its named dependency. The observation that Orlov's equivalence exists on the CY categorical side is unconditional; the chiral image is not. For the quintic the two sides of the correspondence are the LG source $\MF^{\gr}(W_{\mathrm{quintic}})_0$ and the CY source $D^b(\Coh(Q))$ studied in the sister chapter~\ref{ch:derived-cy}.
 \end{remark}
 
 The LG/CY correspondence is the derived-category image of the physics claim that the Landau--Ginzburg theory with superpotential $W$ and the non-linear sigma model on $Q$ are the two phases of a single gauged linear sigma model (Witten \cite{Witten1993}). The Vol~III prediction in Proposition~\ref{prop:lg-cy-matching} is the mathematical assertion that the two phases produce the same chiral algebra, or: that the Vol~III functor $\Phi$ descends to the gauged linear sigma model and does not distinguish its phases.

The discrepancy $5/4 \neq 2$ is the \emph{Enriques $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}$-anomaly}: the BKM weight does not halve under the $\bZ/2$ quotient because the Borcherds product on $O(2, 10)$ is not the restriction of the Igusa cusp form on $O(2, 18)$ but rather an independent automorphic form (the Allcock product) whose weight is determined by the Enriques lattice.

 Five independent verifications of $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}}(K3 \times E) = 5$:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)}]

 \chapter{Cyclic $\Ainf$-Structures}
 \label{ch:cyclic-ainf}
 
-A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in Theorem~CY-A.
+A Calabi--Yau category enters this volume through a single structural datum: a cyclic $\Ainf$-algebra of dimension $d$. Everything that follows, the functor $\Phi$ to chiral algebras, the modular characteristic $\kappa_{\mathrm{cat}}$, the four subscripted kappas of the CY kappa-spectrum, depends on this input. This chapter fixes the definitions, records the standard examples (elliptic curve, K3, quintic), and states the bridge to \ref{part:bridge} precisely. The content is classical (Stasheff, Kontsevich, Keller, Costello); the Vol~III role is the specific identification of $d$ with the CY dimension appearing in the CY-A programme.

 \label{sec:main-results}
 
 \begin{itemize}
- \item \textbf{Theorem CY-A} (CY-to-chiral functor): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
+ \item \textbf{CY-A} (Theorem~CY-A$_2$ / Conjecture~CY-A$_3$): Construction of $\Phi \colon \CY_d\text{-}\Cat \to E_2\text{-}\mathrm{ChirAlg}$ via the factorization envelope and $\mathbb{S}^d$-framing. \emph{Proved for $d = 2$} (CY surfaces, where the $\mathbb{S}^2$-framing gives $E_2$ via Kontsevich--Vlassopoulos). \emph{For $d = 3$}: a programme conditional on (a)~constructing the chain-level $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing compatible with BV structure and (b)~the quantization step. The $\mathbb{S}^3$-framing gives an $E_3$-structure, from which $E_2$ is obtained by restriction; however, $\pi_1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\mathbb{R}^3))$ is trivial, so the restriction gives a symmetric braiding at the topological level. The quantum group braiding for $d = 3$ is expected to arise through the Drinfeld center of $E_1$-monoidal categories.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-B} ($E_2$-chiral Koszul duality; \emph{conditional}): Bar-cobar adjunction in the $E_2$-chiral setting. Items (i)--(ii) follow from the Volume~I machine applied to each $E_1$-direction via Dunn additivity; the full $E_2$-equivariant refinement (coherent intertwining by the $R$-matrix) is established as a rigorous proof sketch, with detailed verification of higher coherences deferred. In the CY$_3$ applications, the further identification of automorphic correction with the shadow obstruction tower and of the denominator identity with the bar-complex Euler product remains a conjectural target rather than a proved consequence.
  \item \textbf{Conjecture CY-C} (Quantum group realization): For toric CY3, $\Rep^{E_2}(G(X))$ should be braided monoidal equivalent to $\Rep(Y(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}}_{Q_X}))$; for $K3 \times E$, the $\Sp_4(\mathbb{Z})$-module structure on the denominator identity should recover the braided structure. The critical CoHA is the $E_1$-sector (positive half). The CY category $\cC(\frakg, q)$ is not yet constructed in general.
  \item \textbf{Theorem CY-D} (Modular CY characteristic): For $d = 2$, $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}(\Phi(\cC))$ equals the CY Euler characteristic $\chi^{\CY}(\cC)$. For $K3 \times E$ ($d = 3$), the Borcherds weight gives the distinct automorphic invariant $\kappa_{\mathrm{BKM}} = 5$; without $A_{K3 \times E}$ (which is not constructed), this is an observation matching the Borcherds lift weight formula, not a computation from the Volume~I definition of $\kappa_{\mathrm{ch}}$. The general $d = 3$ formula is conjectural.
